USERMESSAGE #8
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→# AI Aquarium — QA Test Suite
     2→
     3→Playwright regression tests for [AI Aquarium](https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app).
     4→
     5→Tests run against the live deployment and cover all M1 features.
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## Setup
    10→
    11→```bash
    12→npm install
    13→npx playwright install chromium
    14→```
    15→
    16→---
    17→
    18→## Running Tests
    19→
    20→### Full gate (smoke → regression → critical)
    21→
    22→```bash
    23→npx playwright test
    24→```
    25→
    26→### By tier
    27→
    28→```bash
    29→npm run test:smoke       # Fast smoke (8 tests, ~15s)
    30→npm run test:regression  # Full regression suite
    31→npm run test:critical    # Critical loop integrity
    32→```
    33→
    34→### Specific spec files
    35→
    36→```bash
    37→# M1 debug dashboard
    38→npx playwright test tests/regression/m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js
    39→
    40→# M1 activity duration + AI cycle
    41→npx playwright test tests/regression/m1-activity-duration.spec.js
    42→
    43→# Slot system / capacity
    44→npx playwright test tests/regression/slot-system.spec.js
    45→
    46→# Talk handshaking
    47→npx playwright test tests/regression/talk-handshaking.spec.js
    48→
    49→# Fallback logic
    50→npx playwright test tests/regression/fallback-logic.spec.js
    51→```
    52→
    53→### With UI (for debugging)
    54→
    55→```bash
    56→npx playwright test --ui
    57→```
    58→
    59→---
    60→
    61→## Configuration
    62→
    63→| Setting | Default | Override |
    64→|---------|---------|----------|
    65→| `BASE_URL` | `https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app` | `.env` file or env var |
    66→| Timeout | 60s per test | `playwright.config.js` |
    67→| Retries | 1 | `playwright.config.js` |
    68→
    69→To run against a local or staging deployment:
    70→
    71→```bash
    72→BASE_URL=http://localhost:3000 npx playwright test
    73→```
    74→
    75→---
    76→
    77→## Test Structure
    78→
    79→```
    80→tests/
    81→├── helpers.js                          # Shared helpers (waitForEngine, pollEngineState, waitForDashboard)
    82→├── smoke/
    83→│   └── app-loads.spec.js               # Page loads, engine init, 3 characters, 10 objects
    84→├── regression/
    85→│   ├── ai-decisions.spec.js            # AI LLM requests, valid action types, 30s interval, dialogue fields
    86→│   ├── character-movement.spec.js      # A* pathfinding, tile movement, bounds check
    87→│   ├── object-interaction.spec.js      # Object state transitions, debug panel
    88→│   ├── speech-bubbles.spec.js          # Dialogue lines, pendingSpeech, speech timer
    89→│   ├── m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js      # Debug page layout, real-time updates, pause/resume,
    90→│   │                                   #   position/facing/target fields, reset behavior, speed controls
    91→│   ├── m1-activity-duration.spec.js    # Activity timers, 30s AI cycle, action types
    92→│   ├── slot-system.spec.js             # Slot/capacity display, pip indicators, reservations,
    93→│   │                                   #   multi-capacity simultaneous use (kotatsu/tv)
    94→│   ├── talk-handshaking.spec.js        # TALK/REJECT events, talking state, canTalk filter
    95→│   └── fallback-logic.spec.js          # FALLBACK events, plan changes, reservation release
    96→└── critical/
    97→    └── sim-loop-integrity.spec.js      # End-to-end: LLM → move → object change → no errors
    98→```
    99→
   100→---
   101→
   102→## Feature Coverage Map
   103→
   104→| M1 Feature | Spec File(s) |
   105→|------------|-------------|
   106→| Characters: Maple, Sol, Nyx | `smoke/app-loads.spec.js`, `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
   107→| AI decisions every ~30s | `ai-decisions.spec.js`, `m1-activity-duration.spec.js` |
   108→| Action types: move, interact, idle, talk | `ai-decisions.spec.js`, `m1-activity-duration.spec.js` |
   109→| LLM response: dialogue + description fields | `ai-decisions.spec.js` |
   110→| A* pathfinding, bounds | `character-movement.spec.js`, `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
   111→| Object state machines (idle → active → idle) | `object-interaction.spec.js`, `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
   112→| Activity hold timers | `m1-activity-duration.spec.js`, `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
   113→| Smart objects slot system (capacity, pips) | `slot-system.spec.js` |
   114→| Multi-capacity simultaneous use (kotatsu, tv) | `slot-system.spec.js` |
   115→| Talk handshaking (TALK/REJECT events) | `talk-handshaking.spec.js` |
   116→| Fallback logic (FALLBACK events) | `fallback-logic.spec.js` |
   117→| Debug dashboard: layout + real-time | `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
   118→| Debug dashboard: position, facing, target fields | `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
   119→| Speed controls (functional 2x/4x verify) | `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
   120→| Reset button (behavior, not just visibility) | `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
   121→| Full sim loop integrity | `critical/sim-loop-integrity.spec.js` |
   122→
   123→---
   124→
   125→## CI (GitHub Actions)
   126→
   127→Tests run automatically on every push and pull request via `.github/workflows/playwright.yml`.
   128→
   129→Results are stored as artifacts (JSON report + test-results folder).
   130→
   131→To run CI locally with the same config:
   132→
   133→```bash
   134→npm ci
   135→npx playwright install --with-deps chromium
   136→npx playwright test
   137→```
   138→
   139→---
   140→
   141→## Known Issues
   142→
   143→| Issue | File | Notes |
   144→|-------|------|-------|
   145→| `talk` action rarely observed in short runs | `talk-handshaking.spec.js` | AI is non-deterministic; tests gracefully skip if not observed |
   146→| REJECT events are probabilistic | `talk-handshaking.spec.js` | Only fires when two characters attempt simultaneous talk |
   147→| FALLBACK events require 30s+ observation | `fallback-logic.spec.js` | Events appear after AI cycle + object contention |
   148→| Sim clock ratio ~36x in headless (vs 60x in browser) | `critical/sim-loop-integrity.spec.js` | RAF throttled in headless — documented, not a product bug |
   149→
   150→---
   151→
   152→## Test Results (last run: 2026-03-08)
   153→
   154→| Suite | Tests | Status |
   155→|-------|-------|--------|
   156→| Smoke | 8 | ✅ 8/8 |
   157→| Regression (ai-decisions) | 6 | ✅ 6/6 |
   158→| Regression (character-movement) | 4 | ✅ 4/4 |
   159→| Regression (object-interaction) | 5 | ✅ 5/5 |
   160→| Regression (speech-bubbles) | 4 | ✅ 4/4 |
   161→| Regression (m1-debug-dashboard) | 24 | ✅ 24/24 |
   162→| Regression (m1-activity-duration) | 10 | ✅ 10/10 |
   163→| Regression (slot-system) | 11 | ✅ 11/11 |
   164→| Regression (talk-handshaking) | 6 | ✅ 6/6 |
   165→| Regression (fallback-logic) | 5 | ✅ 5/5 |
   166→| Critical | 5 | ✅ 5/5 |
   167→| **Total** | **88** | **✅ 88/88** |
   168→

USERMESSAGE #12
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→'use strict';
     2→/**
     3→ * Shared helpers for AI Aquarium QA tests.
     4→ * All tests run against the deployed BASE_URL via window._gameEngine.
     5→ */
     6→
     7→/**
     8→ * Wait for Phaser to finish booting and the SimulationEngine to be attached.
     9→ * @param {import('@playwright/test').Page} page
    10→ * @param {number} [timeout=20000]
    11→ */
    12→async function waitForEngine(page, timeout = 20_000) {
    13→  await page.waitForFunction(
    14→    () => window._gameEngine &&
    15→          window._gameEngine.characters &&
    16→          window._gameEngine.characters.length === 3,
    17→    { timeout }
    18→  );
    19→}
    20→
    21→/**
    22→ * Read the live engine state.
    23→ * @param {import('@playwright/test').Page} page
    24→ */
    25→async function getEngineState(page) {
    26→  return page.evaluate(() => {
    27→    const eng = window._gameEngine;
    28→    return {
    29→      simTime: eng.getSimTimeString(),
    30→      aiEnabled: eng.aiEngine?.enabled ?? false,
    31→      characters: eng.characters.map(c => ({
    32→        name: c.name,
    33→        label: c.label,
    34→        tx: c.tx,
    35→        ty: c.ty,
    36→        action: c.action,
    37→        mood: c.mood,
    38→        energy: c.energy,
    39→      })),
    40→      objectStates: eng.objectEngine.getAll().map(o => ({
    41→        id: o.id,
    42→        label: o.label,
    43→        state: o.state,
    44→      })),
    45→    };
    46→  });
    47→}
    48→
    49→/**
    50→ * Poll engine state until predicate returns truthy, or timeout.
    51→ * @param {import('@playwright/test').Page} page
    52→ * @param {(state: object) => boolean} predicate
    53→ * @param {number} [timeoutMs=30000]
    54→ * @param {number} [intervalMs=500]
    55→ */
    56→async function pollEngineState(page, predicate, timeoutMs = 30_000, intervalMs = 500) {
    57→  const deadline = Date.now() + timeoutMs;
    58→  while (Date.now() < deadline) {
    59→    const state = await getEngineState(page);
    60→    if (predicate(state)) return state;
    61→    await page.waitForTimeout(intervalMs);
    62→  }
    63→  throw new Error(`pollEngineState timed out after ${timeoutMs}ms`);
    64→}
    65→
    66→/**
    67→ * Wait for /debug.html dashboard to finish loading.
    68→ * Shared by m1-debug-dashboard, slot-system, talk-handshaking, fallback-logic specs.
    69→ * @param {import('@playwright/test').Page} page
    70→ * @param {number} [timeout=20000]
    71→ */
    72→async function waitForDashboard(page, timeout = 20_000) {
    73→  await page.waitForSelector('#characters-grid', { timeout });
    74→  await page.waitForSelector('#event-log', { timeout });
    75→  await page.waitForFunction(
    76→    () => {
    77→      const cards = document.querySelectorAll('[id^="char-"]');
    78→      if (cards.length < 3) return false;
    79→      return cards[0].textContent.includes('Action');
    80→    },
    81→    { timeout }
    82→  );
    83→}
    84→
    85→module.exports = { waitForEngine, getEngineState, pollEngineState, waitForDashboard };
    86→

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

[intervalMs=500]\n    55\u2192 */\n    56\u2192async function pollEngineState(page, predicate, timeoutMs = 30_000, intervalMs = 500) {\n    57\u2192  const deadline = Date.now() + timeoutMs;\n page.waitForTimeout(intervalMs);\n    62\u2192  }\n    63\u2192  throw new Error(`pollEngineState timed out after ${timeoutMs}ms`);\n    64\u2192}\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192/**\n    67\u2192 * Wait for /debug.html dashboard to finish loading.\n    68\u2192 * Shared by m1-debug-dashboard, slot-system, talk-handshaking, fallback-logic specs.\n    69\u2192 * @param {import('@playwright/test').Page} page\n    70\u2192 * @param {number} [timeout=20000]\n    71\u2192 */\n    72\u2192async function waitForDashboard(page, timeout = 20_000) {\n    73\u2192  await page.waitForSelector('#characters-grid', { timeout });\n    74\u2192  await page.waitForSelector('#event-log', { timeout });\n    75\u2192  await page.waitForFunction(\n    76\u2192    () => {\n USERMESSAGE #19
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→'use strict';
     2→/**
     3→ * REGRESSION — FRG-234
     4→ * Talk Handshaking
     5→ *
     6→ * Verifies the character-to-character conversation system:
     7→ *  - Characters can initiate talk via AI action: 'talk'
     8→ *  - On accept: both enter talking state, TALK event appears in event log
     9→ *  - On reject: REJECT event appears in event log with reason
    10→ *  - canTalk filter: LLM receives canTalk status for nearby characters
    11→ *  - Talk targets observed across all characters over time
    12→ *
    13→ * Target: /debug.html (for event log) and / (for engine state)
    14→ */
    15→const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');
    16→const { waitForEngine, pollEngineState, waitForDashboard } = require('../helpers');
    17→
    18→test.describe('M1: Talk Handshaking', () => {
    19→  // ── TALK EVENTS IN EVENT LOG ──────────────────────────────────────────────
    20→
    21→  test('TALK event appears in event log within 90 seconds', async ({ page }) => {
    22→    test.setTimeout(120_000);
    23→
    24→    await page.goto('/debug.html');
    25→    await waitForDashboard(page);
    26→
    27→    // Enable 4x speed to accelerate AI cycles
    28→    await page.getByRole('button', { name: '4x' }).click();
    29→
    30→    await page.waitForFunction(
    31→      () => document.querySelector('.log-entry.talk') !== null,
    32→      { timeout: 90_000, polling: 1000 }
    33→    );
    34→
    35→    const talkEntries = await page.evaluate(() => {
    36→      const entries = document.querySelectorAll('.log-entry.talk');
    37→      return Array.from(entries).map(e => ({
    38→        obj: e.querySelector('.log-obj')?.textContent.trim(),
    39→        transition: e.querySelector('.log-transition')?.textContent.trim(),
    40→        tag: e.querySelector('.log-tag')?.textContent.trim(),
    41→      }));
    42→    });
    43→
    44→    expect(talkEntries.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
    45→    for (const entry of talkEntries) {
    46→      expect(entry.tag).toBe('TALK');
    47→      // Transition should show "talking ↔ CharacterName"
    48→      expect(entry.transition).toMatch(/talking\s*↔\s*(Maple|Sol|Nyx)/);
    49→      // obj should be a character name
    50→      expect(['Maple', 'Sol', 'Nyx']).toContain(entry.obj);
    51→    }
    52→    console.log('TALK events found:', talkEntries);
    53→  });
    54→
    55→  test('AI decision with action=talk appears in event log', async ({ page }) => {
    56→    test.setTimeout(120_000);
    57→
    58→    await page.goto('/debug.html');
    59→    await waitForDashboard(page);
    60→
    61→    // Enable 4x speed
    62→    await page.getByRole('button', { name: '4x' }).click();
    63→
    64→    // Wait for an AI event with "talk →" transition
    65→    await page.waitForFunction(
    66→      () => {
    67→        const entries = document.querySelectorAll('.log-entry.ai');
    68→        return Array.from(entries).some(e => {
    69→          const t = e.querySelector('.log-transition');
    70→          return t && t.textContent.includes('talk →');
    71→        });
    72→      },
    73→      { timeout: 90_000, polling: 1000 }
    74→    );
    75→
    76→    const talkAiEntries = await page.evaluate(() => {
    77→      const entries = document.querySelectorAll('.log-entry.ai');
    78→      return Array.from(entries)
    79→        .filter(e => e.querySelector('.log-transition')?.textContent.includes('talk →'))
    80→        .map(e => ({
    81→          obj: e.querySelector('.log-obj')?.textContent.trim(),
    82→          transition: e.querySelector('.log-transition')?.textContent.trim(),
    83→        }));
    84→    });
    85→
    86→    expect(talkAiEntries.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
    87→    for (const entry of talkAiEntries) {
    88→      expect(['Maple', 'Sol', 'Nyx']).toContain(entry.obj);
    89→      expect(entry.transition).toMatch(/talk → (Maple|Sol|Nyx)/);
    90→    }
    91→    console.log('AI talk decisions:', talkAiEntries);
    92→  });
    93→
    94→  // ── REJECT EVENTS ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    95→
    96→  test('REJECT event appears in event log when character is busy', async ({ page }) => {
    97→    test.setTimeout(180_000);
    98→
    99→    await page.goto('/debug.html');
   100→    await waitForDashboard(page);
   101→
   102→    // Enable 4x speed to see more AI cycles
   103→    await page.getByRole('button', { name: '4x' }).click();
   104→
   105→    // REJECT events are probabilistic — they only fire when someone tries to talk
   106→    // to a character who is interacting or already talking.
   107→    // Wait up to 2.5 minutes (covers ~5 AI cycles at 30s each)
   108→    const found = await page.waitForFunction(
   109→      () => document.querySelector('.log-entry.reject') !== null,
   110→      { timeout: 150_000, polling: 1000 }
   111→    ).catch(() => null);
   112→
   113→    if (!found) {
   114→      // REJECT events only happen when characters happen to conflict.
   115→      // Log a note but don't fail — this is probabilistic, not a product bug.
   116→      console.log('[SKIP] No REJECT events observed in this run (probabilistic — requires talk attempt on busy character)');
   117→      return;
   118→    }
   119→
   120→    const rejectEntries = await page.evaluate(() => {
   121→      const entries = document.querySelectorAll('.log-entry.reject');
   122→      return Array.from(entries).map(e => ({
   123→        obj: e.querySelector('.log-obj')?.textContent.trim(),
   124→        transition: e.querySelector('.log-transition')?.textContent.trim(),
   125→        tag: e.querySelector('.log-tag')?.textContent.trim(),
   126→      }));
   127→    });
   128→
   129→    expect(rejectEntries.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
   130→    for (const entry of rejectEntries) {
   131→      expect(entry.tag).toBe('REJECT');
   132→      // Transition shows "→ CharacterName rejected (reason)"
   133→      expect(entry.transition).toMatch(/→\s*(Maple|Sol|Nyx)\s+rejected/);
   134→    }
   135→    console.log('REJECT events found:', rejectEntries);
   136→  });
   137→
   138→  // ── CAN-TALK FILTER ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   139→
   140→  test('LLM request body includes canTalk status for nearby characters', async ({ page }) => {
   141→    const requestBodies = [];
   142→
   143→    page.on('request', req => {
   144→      if (req.url().includes('/api/llm') && req.method() === 'POST') {
   145→        try {
   146→          requestBodies.push(JSON.parse(req.postData() || '{}'));
   147→        } catch { /* ignore */ }
   148→      }
   149→    });
   150→
   151→    await page.goto('/');
   152→    await page.waitForFunction(
   153→      () => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3,
   154→      { timeout: 20_000 }
   155→    );
   156→    await page.waitForTimeout(10_000);
   157→
   158→    // At least some requests should include character info with canTalk
   159→    const withCanTalk = requestBodies.filter(body => {
   160→      const content = body.messages?.[0]?.content || '';
   161→      return content.includes('canTalk');
   162→    });
   163→
   164→    if (withCanTalk.length === 0) {
   165→      // canTalk may be in the system prompt instead of the user message
   166→      const withNearbyChars = requestBodies.filter(body => {
   167→        const sys = body.system || '';
   168→        const user = body.messages?.[0]?.content || '';
   169→        return sys.includes('canTalk') || user.includes('Nearby characters') || user.includes('characters');
   170→      });
   171→      expect(withNearbyChars.length, 'LLM requests should include character availability info').toBeGreaterThan(0);
   172→      console.log('Character info in LLM requests (system or user message):', withNearbyChars.length);
   173→    } else {
   174→      expect(withCanTalk.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);
   175→      console.log('LLM requests with canTalk field:', withCanTalk.length);
   176→    }
   177→  });
   178→
   179→  // ── CHARACTERS ENTER TALKING STATE ────────────────────────────────────────
   180→
   181→  test('characters enter talking action state during conversation', async ({ page }) => {
   182→    test.setTimeout(120_000);
   183→
   184→    await page.goto('/');
   185→    await page.waitForFunction(
   186→      () => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3,
   187→      { timeout: 20_000 }
   188→    );
   189→
   190→    // Poll engine state for any character in talking state
   191→    const talkState = await pollEngineState(
   192→      page,
   193→      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'talking'),
   194→      90_000,
   195→      500
   196→    ).catch(() => null);
   197→
   198→    if (!talkState) {
   199→      console.log('[SKIP] No talking state observed in this run (AI may not have chosen talk action)');
   200→      return;
   201→    }
   202→
   203→    const talkers = talkState.characters.filter(c => c.action === 'talking');
   204→    expect(talkers.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1);
   205→    console.log('Characters in talking state:', talkers.map(c => c.label).join(', '));
   206→  });
   207→
   208→  // ── TALK TARGETS DISTRIBUTED ACROSS CHARACTERS ───────────────────────────
   209→
   210→  test('talk action targets distributed across multiple characters over time', async ({ page }) => {
   211→    test.setTimeout(120_000);
   212→
   213→    const talkTargets = new Set();
   214→
   215→    page.on('response', async res => {
   216→      if (res.url().includes('/api/llm') && res.request().method() === 'POST') {
   217→        try {
   218→          const json = await res.json();
   219→          const text = json?.content?.[0]?.text ?? json?.choices?.[0]?.message?.content;
   220→          if (text) {
   221→            const d = JSON.parse(text);
   222→            if (d.action === 'talk' && d.targetCharacter) {
   223→              talkTargets.add(d.targetCharacter);
   224→            }
   225→          }
   226→        } catch { /* ignore */ }
   227→      }
   228→    });
   229→
   230→    await page.goto('/');
   231→    await page.waitForFunction(
   232→      () => window._gameEngine?.characters?.length === 3,
   233→      { timeout: 20_000 }
   234→    );
   235→    await page.waitForTimeout(90_000);
   236→
   237→    if (talkTargets.size === 0) {
   238→      console.log('[INFO] No talk actions observed in this run — AI chose other actions');
   239→      // Not a failure: AI is non-deterministic. The LLM responses valid action test already covers this.
   240→      return;
   241→    }
   242→
   243→    // If talk actions were observed, targets should be valid character names
   244→    for (const target of talkTargets) {
   245→      expect(['Maple', 'Sol', 'Nyx']).toContain(target);
   246→    }
   247→    console.log('Talk targets observed:', [...talkTargets]);
   248→  });
   249→});
   250→

character-to-character conversation system:\n     7\u2192 *  - Characters can initiate talk via AI action: 'talk'\n     8\u2192 *  - On accept: both enter talking state, TALK event appears in event log\n     9\u2192 *  - On reject: REJECT event appears in event log with reason\n    10\u2192 *  - canTalk filter: LLM receives canTalk status for nearby characters\n    11\u2192 *  - Talk targets observed across all characters over time\n    12\u2192 *\n    13\u2192 * Target: /debug.html (for event log) and / (for engine state)\n    14\u2192 */\n    15\u2192const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');\n    16\u2192const { waitForEngine, pollEngineState, waitForDashboard } = require('../helpers');\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192test.describe('M1: Talk Handshaking', () => {\n    19\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 TALK EVENTS IN EVENT LOG \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192  test('TALK event appears in event log within 90 seconds', async ({ page }) => {\n    22\u2192    test.setTimeout(120_000);\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192    await page.goto('/debug.html');\n    25\u2192    await waitForDashboard(page);\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192    // Enable 4x speed to accelerate AI cycles\n    28\u2192    await page.getByRole('button', { name: '4x' }).click();\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192    await page.waitForFunction(\n    31\u2192      () => document.querySelector('.log-entry.talk') !== null,\n    32\u2192      { timeout: 90_000, polling: 1000 }\n    33\u2192    );\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192    const talkEntries = await page.evaluate(() => {\n    36\u2192      const entries = document.querySelectorAll('.log-entry.talk');\n    37\u2192      return Array.from(entries).map(e => ({\n    38\u2192        obj: e.querySelector('.log-obj')?.textContent.trim(),\n    39\u2192        transition: e.querySelector('.log-transition')?.textContent.trim(),\n    40\u2192        tag: e.querySelector('.log-tag')?.textContent.trim(),\n    41\u2192      }));\n    42\u2192    });\n    43\u2192\n    44\u2192    expect(talkEntries.length).toBeGreaterThan(0);\n    45\u2192    for (const entry of talkEntries) {\n    46\u2192      expect(entry.tag).toBe('TALK');\n    47\u2192      // Transition should show \"talking \u2194 CharacterName\"\n    48\u2192      expect(entry.transition).toMatch(/talking\\s*\u2194\\s*(Maple|Sol|Nyx)/);\n    49\u2192      // obj should be a character name\n    50\u2192      expect(['Maple', 'Sol', 'Nyx']).toContain(entry.obj);\n    51\u2192    }\n    52\u2192    console.log('TALK events found:', talkEntries);\n    53\u2192  });\n    54\u2192\n    55\u2192  test('AI decision with action=talk appears in event log', async ({ page }) => {\n    56\u2192    test.setTimeout(120_000);\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192    await page.goto('/debug.html');\n    59\u2192    await waitForDashboard(page);\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192    // Enable 4x speed\n    62\u2192    await page.getByRole('button', { name: '4x' }).click();\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192    // Wait for an    95\u2192\n    96\u2192  test('REJECT event appears in event log when character is busy', async ({ page }) => {\n    97\u2192    test.setTimeout(180_000);\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192    await USERMESSAGE #21
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→'use strict';
     2→/**
     3→ * REGRESSION — FRG-184
     4→ * Verifies that characters move via A* pathfinding (action = 'walking',
     5→ * tile coordinates change over time).
     6→ */
     7→const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');
     8→const { waitForEngine, getEngineState, pollEngineState } = require('../helpers');
     9→
    10→test.describe('Regression: Character movement via A*', () => {
    11→  test.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {
    12→    await page.goto('/');
    13→    await waitForEngine(page);
    14→  });
    15→
    16→  test('at least one character enters walking state within 15 seconds', async ({ page }) => {
    17→    const finalState = await pollEngineState(
    18→      page,
    19→      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'walking'),
    20→      15_000
    21→    );
    22→    const walking = finalState.characters.filter(c => c.action === 'walking');
    23→    expect(walking.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1);
    24→  });
    25→
    26→  test('walking character tile position changes over time', async ({ page }) => {
    27→    // Wait until someone walks
    28→    const state1 = await pollEngineState(
    29→      page,
    30→      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'walking'),
    31→      15_000
    32→    );
    33→
    34→    const walker = state1.characters.find(c => c.action === 'walking');
    35→    const startTx = walker.tx;
    36→    const startTy = walker.ty;
    37→
    38→    // Wait for movement to complete
    39→    await page.waitForTimeout(6000);
    40→    const state2 = await getEngineState(page);
    41→    const walkerAfter = state2.characters.find(c => c.name === walker.name);
    42→
    43→    // Position must have changed
    44→    const moved = walkerAfter.tx !== startTx || walkerAfter.ty !== startTy;
    45→    expect(moved, `${walker.label} did not move from (${startTx}, ${startTy})`).toBe(true);
    46→  });
    47→
    48→  test('characters stay within walkable bounds (cols 1–17, rows 2–9)', async ({ page }) => {
    49→    // Sample character positions over 10 seconds
    50→    for (let i = 0; i < 5; i++) {
    51→      await page.waitForTimeout(2000);
    52→      const state = await getEngineState(page);
    53→      for (const c of state.characters) {
    54→        expect(c.tx, `${c.label} x=${c.tx} out of bounds`).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1);
    55→        expect(c.tx, `${c.label} x=${c.tx} out of bounds`).toBeLessThanOrEqual(17);
    56→        expect(c.ty, `${c.label} y=${c.ty} out of bounds`).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(2);
    57→        expect(c.ty, `${c.label} y=${c.ty} out of bounds`).toBeLessThanOrEqual(9);
    58→      }
    59→    }
    60→  });
    61→
    62→  test('character action transitions: idle → walking → interacting or idle', async ({ page }) => {
    63→    // Observe a character through a full walk cycle.
    64→    // Characters often go walking → interacting (wander timer fires activity on arrival)
    65→    // so we accept either interacting or idle as the post-walk state.
    66→    const walkStartState = await pollEngineState(
    67→      page,
    68→      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'walking'),
    69→      15_000
    70→    );
    71→    const charName = walkStartState.characters.find(c => c.action === 'walking').name;
    72→
    73→    // Wait for that character to stop walking (arrive at destination)
    74→    const arrivedState = await pollEngineState(
    75→      page,
    76→      state => {
    77→        const c = state.characters.find(c => c.name === charName);
    78→        return c && c.action !== 'walking';
    79→      },
    80→      40_000
    81→    );
    82→    const charArrived = arrivedState.characters.find(c => c.name === charName);
    83→    expect(['idle', 'interacting', 'talking']).toContain(charArrived.action);
    84→  });
    85→});
    86→

{

    "tool_use_id": "toolu_01HxKYTgPMTCu2QaoKtDEM7V",
    "type": "tool_result",
    "content": "     1\u2192'use strict';\n     2\u2192/**\n     3\u2192 * REGRESSION \u2014 FRG-184\n     4\u2192 * Verifies that characters move via A* pathfinding (action = 'walking',\n     5\u2192 * tile coordinates change over time).\n     6\u2192 */\n     7\u2192const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');\n     8\u2192const { waitForEngine, getEngineState, pollEngineState } = require('../helpers');\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192test.describe('Regression: Character movement via A*', () => {\n    11\u2192  test.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {\n    12\u2192    await page.goto('/');\n    13\u2192    await waitForEngine(page);\n    14\u2192  });\n    15\u2192\n    16\u2192  test('at least one character enters walking state within 15 seconds', async ({ page }) => {\n    17\u2192    const finalState = await pollEngineState(\n    18\u2192      page,\n    19\u2192      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'walking'),\n    20\u2192      15_000\n    21\u2192    );\n    22\u2192    const walking = finalState.characters.filter(c => c.action === 'walking');\n    23\u2192    expect(walking.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1);\n    24\u2192  });\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192  test('walking character tile position changes over time', async ({ page }) => {\n    27\u2192    // Wait until someone walks\n    28\u2192    const state1 = await pollEngineState(\n    29\u2192      page,\n    30\u2192      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'walking'),\n    31\u2192      15_000\n    32\u2192    );\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192    const walker = state1.characters.find(c => c.action === 'walking');\n    35\u2192    const startTx = walker.tx;\n    36\u2192    const startTy = walker.ty;\n    37\u2192\n    38\u2192    // Wait for movement to complete\n    39\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(6000);\n    40\u2192    const state2 = await getEngineState(page);\n    41\u2192    const walkerAfter = state2.characters.find(c => c.name === walker.name);\n    42\u2192\n    43\u2192    // Position must have changed\n    44\u2192    const moved = walkerAfter.tx !== startTx || walkerAfter.ty !== startTy;\n    45\u2192    expect(moved, `${walker.label} did not move from (${startTx}, ${startTy})`).toBe(true);\n    46\u2192  });\n    47\u2192\n    48\u2192  test('characters stay within walkable bounds (cols 1\u201317, rows 2\u20139)', async ({ page }) => {\n    49\u2192    // Sample character positions over 10 seconds\n    50\u2192    for (let i = 0; i < 5; i++) {\n    51\u2192      await page.waitForTimeout(2000);\n    52\u2192      const state = await getEngineState(page);\n    53\u2192      for (const c of state.characters) {\n    54\u2192        expect(c.tx, `${c.label} x=${c.tx} out of bounds`).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1);\n    55\u2192        expect(c.tx, `${c.label} x=${c.tx} out of bounds`).toBeLessThanOrEqual(17);\n    56\u2192        expect(c.ty, `${c.label} y=${c.ty} out of bounds`).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(2);\n    57\u2192        expect(c.ty, `${c.label} y=${c.ty} out of bounds`).toBeLessThanOrEqual(9);\n    58\u2192      }\n    59\u2192    }\n    60\u2192  });\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192  test('character action transitions: idle \u2192 walking \u2192 interacting or idle', async ({ page }) => {\n    63\u2192    // Observe a character through a full walk cycle.\n    64\u2192    // Characters often go walking \u2192 interacting (wander timer fires activity on arrival)\n    65\u2192    // so we accept either interacting or idle as the post-walk state.\n    66\u2192    const walkStartState = await pollEngineState(\n    67\u2192      page,\n    68\u2192      state => state.characters.some(c => c.action === 'walking'),\n    69\u2192      15_000\n    70\u2192    );\n    71\u2192    const charName = walkStartState.characters.find(c => c.action === 'walking').name;\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192    // Wait for that character to stop walking (arrive at destination)\n    74\u2192    const arrivedState = await pollEngineState(\n    75\u2192      page,\n    76\u2192      state => {\n    77\u2192        const c = state.characters.find(c => c.name === charName);\n    78\u2192        return c && c.action !== 'walking';\n    79\u2192      },\n    80\u2192      40_000\n    81\u2192    );\n    82\u2192    const charArrived = arrivedState.characters.find(c => c.name === charName);\n    83\u2192    expect(['idle', 'interacting', 'talking']).toContain(charArrived.action);\n    84\u2192  });\n    85\u2192});\n USERMESSAGE #25
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→'use strict';
     2→/**
     3→ * REGRESSION — FRG-184
     4→ * Verifies that object state changes are visible and that interactions
     5→ * (both random-wander and AI-triggered) work correctly.
     6→ *
     7→ * Also captures the known bug: AIEngine uses `_pendingInteract` but
     8→ * SimulationEngine._handleObjectArrival checks `targetObjectId`, so
     9→ * AI-driven interactions never fire object state transitions.
    10→ */
    11→const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');
    12→const { waitForEngine, getEngineState, pollEngineState } = require('../helpers');
    13→
    14→test.describe('Regression: Object interaction and state changes', () => {
    15→  test.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {
    16→    await page.goto('/');
    17→    await waitForEngine(page);
    18→  });
    19→
    20→  test('at least one object changes state within 60 seconds', async ({ page }) => {
    21→    const initial = await getEngineState(page);
    22→    const initialMap = Object.fromEntries(initial.objectStates.map(o => [o.id, o.state]));
    23→
    24→    const changed = await pollEngineState(
    25→      page,
    26→      state => state.objectStates.some(o => o.state !== initialMap[o.id]),
    27→      60_000,
    28→      1000
    29→    );
    30→
    31→    const changedObjects = changed.objectStates.filter(o => o.state !== initialMap[o.id]);
    32→    expect(changedObjects.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1);
    33→    console.log('Objects that changed state:', changedObjects.map(o => `${o.id}: ${initialMap[o.id]} → ${o.state}`).join(', '));
    34→  });
    35→
    36→  test('debug panel reflects live object state changes', async ({ page }) => {
    37→    // Open debug panel
    38→    await page.getByRole('button', { name: /debug/i }).click();
    39→
    40→    // Wait for an object to go non-idle
    41→    const changed = await pollEngineState(
    42→      page,
    43→      state => state.objectStates.some(o => o.state !== 'idle'),
    44→      60_000,
    45→      1000
    46→    );
    47→
    48→    const nonIdle = changed.objectStates.find(o => o.state !== 'idle');
    49→    expect(nonIdle).toBeTruthy();
    50→
    51→    // The debug panel should reflect the new state
    52→    const stateText = page.getByText(nonIdle.state, { exact: false });
    53→    await expect(stateText).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5000 });
    54→  });
    55→
    56→  test('debug panel "use" button triggers object state change', async ({ page }) => {
    57→    await page.getByRole('button', { name: /debug/i }).click();
    58→
    59→    const initial = await getEngineState(page);
    60→    const idleObject = initial.objectStates.find(o => o.state === 'idle');
    61→    expect(idleObject, 'Need at least one idle object').toBeTruthy();
    62→
    63→    // Find and click the "use" button for that object
    64→    // The debug panel rows are: ObjectName | stateLabel | useButton
    65→    const objectLabel = idleObject.label;
    66→    const row = page.locator(`text=${objectLabel}`).locator('..').first();
    67→    const useBtn = row.getByRole('button', { name: /use/i });
    68→
    69→    // Fall back: just find any use button
    70→    const anyUseBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: 'use' }).first();
    71→    await anyUseBtn.click();
    72→
    73→    // State should change
    74→    await page.waitForTimeout(500);
    75→    const after = await getEngineState(page);
    76→    const anyNonIdle = after.objectStates.some(o => o.state !== 'idle');
    77→    expect(anyNonIdle, 'No object changed state after clicking "use"').toBe(true);
    78→  });
    79→
    80→  test('object auto-transitions back to idle after timed duration', async ({ page }) => {
    81→    // Target the bonsai specifically: auto_next = 12 real seconds (fastest cycle)
    82→    // Test timeout extended to allow the full cycle
    83→    test.setTimeout(120_000);
    84→
    85→    await page.getByRole('button', { name: /debug/i }).click();
    86→
    87→    // Directly trigger bonsai via engine (avoids DOM layout uncertainty)
    88→    await page.evaluate(() => {
    89→      window._gameEngine.objectEngine.interact('bonsai', 'use');
    90→    });
    91→
    92→    // Confirm bonsai is now in 'tending'
    93→    await page.waitForTimeout(500);
    94→    const activated = await getEngineState(page);
    95→    const bonsai = activated.objectStates.find(o => o.id === 'bonsai');
    96→    expect(bonsai?.state, 'Bonsai should be in tending state').toBe('tending');
    97→
    98→    console.log('Bonsai transitioned to tending, waiting for auto-return to idle (12s)...');
    99→
   100→    // Wait for bonsai auto_next: idle after 12 real seconds
   101→    const returned = await pollEngineState(
   102→      page,
   103→      state => state.objectStates.find(o => o.id === 'bonsai')?.state === 'idle',
   104→      30_000,
   105→      500
   106→    );
   107→    const bonsaiAfter = returned.objectStates.find(o => o.id === 'bonsai');
   108→    expect(bonsaiAfter.state).toBe('idle');
   109→  });
   110→
   111→  // ── BUG DOCUMENTATION TEST ────────────────────────────────────────────────
   112→  test('[BUG] AI interact decision sets _pendingInteract but engine checks targetObjectId', async ({ page }) => {
   113→    // This test documents the known bug:
   114→    // AIEngine._applyDecision sets character._pendingInteract = obj.id
   115→    // SimulationEngine._handleObjectArrival checks char.targetObjectId (never set by AI)
   116→    // Result: AI-driven "interact" decisions do NOT trigger object state changes.
   117→
   118→    const interactDecisions = [];
   119→    page.on('response', async res => {
   120→      if (res.url().includes('/api/llm')) {
   121→        try {
   122→          const json = await res.json();
   123→          const text = json?.content?.[0]?.text ?? json?.choices?.[0]?.message?.content;
   124→          if (text) {
   125→            const d = JSON.parse(text);
   126→            if (d.action === 'interact') interactDecisions.push(d);
   127→          }
   128→        } catch { /* ignore */ }
   129→      }
   130→    });
   131→
   132→    await page.goto('/');
   133→    await waitForEngine(page);
   134→    await page.waitForTimeout(10000); // capture initial decisions
   135→
   136→    if (interactDecisions.length === 0) {
   137→      // No interact decisions in this run — test is inconclusive, skip gracefully
   138→      console.log('[BUG test] No "interact" decisions captured in this run, skipping verification');
   139→      return;
   140→    }
   141→
   142→    // Verify: after an AI "interact" decision, check if _pendingInteract is set
   143→    // and if targetObjectId is NOT set (confirming the bug)
   144→    const bugCheck = await page.evaluate(() => {
   145→      const chars = window._gameEngine?.characters ?? [];
   146→      return chars.map(c => ({
   147→        name: c.name,
   148→        hasPendingInteract: '_pendingInteract' in c && c._pendingInteract !== undefined,
   149→        hasTargetObjectId: c.targetObjectId !== null && c.targetObjectId !== undefined,
   150→        pendingInteract: c._pendingInteract,
   151→        targetObjectId: c.targetObjectId,
   152→      }));
   153→    });
   154→
   155→    console.log('[BUG] Character interact state:', JSON.stringify(bugCheck, null, 2));
   156→
   157→    // Document: at least one character with _pendingInteract should lack targetObjectId
   158→    // This confirms the mismatch
   159→    const bugged = bugCheck.filter(c => c.hasPendingInteract && !c.hasTargetObjectId);
   160→    if (bugged.length > 0) {
   161→      console.warn(`[BUG CONFIRMED] ${bugged.length} character(s) have _pendingInteract set but targetObjectId is null — AI interact decisions will not trigger object state changes`);
   162→    }
   163→
   164→    // The test itself passes (it's documenting, not blocking) — the regression
   165→    // suite will catch when this is fixed by expecting AI-triggered state changes.
   166→    expect(interactDecisions.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1); // we got interact decisions
   167→  });
   168→});
   169→

state.objectStates.find(o => o.id === 'bonsai')?.state === 'idle',\n   104\u2192      30_000,\n   105\u2192      500\n   106\u2192    );\n   107\u2192    const bonsaiAfter = returned.objectStates.find(o => o.id === 'bonsai');\n   108\u2192    expect(bonsaiAfter.state).toBe('idle');\n   109\u2192  });\n   110\u2192\n   111\u2192  // \u2500\u2500 BUG DOCUMENTATION TEST \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n   112\u2192  test('[BUG] AI interact decision sets _pendingInteract but engine checks targetObjectId', async ({ page }) => {\n   113\u2192    // This test documents the known bug:\n   114\u2192    // AIEngine._applyDecision sets character._pendingInteract = obj.id\n   115\u2192    // SimulationEngine._handleObjectArrival checks char.targetObjectId (never set by AI)\n   116\u2192    // Result: AI-driven \"interact\" decisions do NOT trigger object state changes.\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192    const interactDecisions = [];\n   119\u2192    page.on('response', async res => {\n   120\u2192      if (res.url().includes('/api/llm')) {\n   121\u2192        try {\n   122\u2192          const json = await res.json();\n   123\u2192          const text = json?.content?.[0]?.text ?? json?.choices?.[0]?.message?.content;\n   124\u2192          if (text) {\n   125\u2192            const d = JSON.parse(text);\n   126\u2192            if (d.action === 'interact') interactDecisions.push(d);\n   127\u2192          }\n   128\u2192        } catch { /* ignore */ }\n   129\u2192      }\n   130\u2192    });\n   131\u2192\n   132\u2192    await page.goto('/');\n   133\u2192    await waitForEngine(page);\n   134\u2192    await page.waitForTimeout(10000); // capture initial decisions\n   135\u2192\n   136\u2192    if (interactDecisions.length === 0) {\n   137\u2192      // No interact decisions in this run \u2014 test is inconclusive, skip gracefully\n   138\u2192      console.log('[BUG test] No \"interact\" decisions captured in this run, skipping verification');\n   139\u2192      return;\n   140\u2192    }\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192    // Verify: after an AI \"interact\" decision, check if _pendingInteract is set\n   143\u2192    // and if targetObjectId is NOT set (confirming the bug)\n   144\u2192    const bugCheck = await page.evaluate(() => {\n   145\u2192      const chars = window._gameEngine?.characters ?? [];\n   146\u2192      return chars.map(c => ({\n   147\u2192        name: c.name,\n   148\u2192        hasPendingInteract: '_pendingInteract' in c && c._pendingInteract !== undefined,\n   149\u2192        hasTargetObjectId: c.targetObjectId !== null && c.targetObjectId !== undefined,\n   150\u2192        pendingInteract: c._pendingInteract,\n   151\u2192        targetObjectId: c.targetObjectId,\n   152\u2192      }));\n   153\u2192    });\n   154\u2192\n   155\u2192    console.log('[BUG] Character interact state:', JSON.stringify(bugCheck, null, 2));\n   156\u2192\n   157\u2192    // Document: at least one character with _pendingInteract should lack targetObjectId\n   158\u2192    // This confirms the mismatch\n   159\u2192    const bugged = bugCheck.filter(c => c.hasPendingInteract && !c.hasTargetObjectId);\n   160\u2192    if (bugged.length > 0) {\n   161\u2192      console.warn(`[BUG CONFIRMED] ${bugged.length} character(s) have _pendingInteract set but targetObjectId is null \u2014 AI interact decisions will not trigger object state changes`);\n   162\u2192    }\n   163\u2192\n   164\u2192    // The test itself passes (it's documenting, not blocking) \u2014 the regression\n   165\u2192    // suite will catch when this is fixed by expecting AI-triggered state changes.\n   166\u2192    expect(interactDecisions.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1); // we got interact decisions\n   167\u2192  });\n   168\u2192});\n   169\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
  }
}

USERMESSAGE #58
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     2→// Character System — Movement, Perception, A* Pathfinding
     3→// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     4→//
     5→// Characters have: id, name, personality, position, action state,
     6→// mood, energy, facing direction. They move between tiles using
     7→// A* pathfinding on the walkable tilemap.
     8→//
     9→// Walk animation alternates walk1/walk2 sprites while moving.
    10→// Perception system returns nearby objects and characters within radius.
    11→
    12→import { MAP, T, MAP_COLS, MAP_ROWS, TILE_SIZE } from './tilemap.js';
    13→
    14→// ── Walkable tiles (everything except walls, fences, water) ──
    15→const UNWALKABLE = new Set([T.WALL, T.WALL_TOP, T.COUNTER, T.COUNTER_L, T.COUNTER_R, T.WINDOW]);
    16→
    17→export function isWalkable(tx, ty) {
    18→  if (tx < 0 || tx >= MAP_COLS || ty < 0 || ty >= MAP_ROWS) return false;
    19→  return !UNWALKABLE.has(MAP[ty][tx]);
    20→}
    21→
    22→// ── A* Pathfinding ──
    23→// Returns array of {tx, ty} waypoints (excludes start, includes goal),
    24→// or null if no path found. Returns [] if already at goal.
    25→
    26→const NEIGHBORS = [[0, -1], [0, 1], [-1, 0], [1, 0]];
    27→
    28→export function findPath(sx, sy, gx, gy) {
    29→  if (!isWalkable(gx, gy)) return null;
    30→
    31→  const startKey = sy * MAP_COLS + sx;
    32→  const goalKey = gy * MAP_COLS + gx;
    33→  if (startKey === goalKey) return [];
    34→
    35→  const open = [startKey];
    36→  const cameFrom = new Map();
    37→  const gScore = new Map([[startKey, 0]]);
    38→  const fScore = new Map([[startKey, Math.abs(sx - gx) + Math.abs(sy - gy)]]);
    39→  const closed = new Set();
    40→
    41→  while (open.length > 0) {
    42→    // Find node with lowest fScore
    43→    let bestIdx = 0;
    44→    let bestF = fScore.get(open[0]) ?? Infinity;
    45→    for (let i = 1; i < open.length; i++) {
    46→      const f = fScore.get(open[i]) ?? Infinity;
    47→      if (f < bestF) { bestF = f; bestIdx = i; }
    48→    }
    49→
    50→    const current = open[bestIdx];
    51→    if (current === goalKey) {
    52→      const path = [];
    53→      let c = current;
    54→      while (cameFrom.has(c)) {
    55→        path.unshift({ tx: c % MAP_COLS, ty: Math.floor(c / MAP_COLS) });
    56→        c = cameFrom.get(c);
    57→      }
    58→      return path;
    59→    }
    60→
    61→    open.splice(bestIdx, 1);
    62→    closed.add(current);
    63→
    64→    const cx = current % MAP_COLS;
    65→    const cy = Math.floor(current / MAP_COLS);
    66→
    67→    for (const [dx, dy] of NEIGHBORS) {
    68→      const nx = cx + dx;
    69→      const ny = cy + dy;
    70→      if (!isWalkable(nx, ny)) continue;
    71→
    72→      const nKey = ny * MAP_COLS + nx;
    73→      if (closed.has(nKey)) continue;
    74→
    75→      const tentG = (gScore.get(current) ?? Infinity) + 1;
    76→      if (tentG < (gScore.get(nKey) ?? Infinity)) {
    77→        cameFrom.set(nKey, current);
    78→        gScore.set(nKey, tentG);
    79→        fScore.set(nKey, tentG + Math.abs(nx - gx) + Math.abs(ny - gy));
    80→        if (!open.includes(nKey)) open.push(nKey);
    81→      }
    82→    }
    83→  }
    84→
    85→  return null; // no path
    86→}
    87→
    88→// ── Find walkable tile adjacent to an object (for interaction) ──
    89→export function findInteractTile(objPosition) {
    90→  const { tx, ty, tw = 1, th = 1 } = objPosition;
    91→  const candidates = [];
    92→
    93→  for (let x = tx - 1; x <= tx + tw; x++) {
    94→    if (isWalkable(x, ty - 1)) candidates.push({ tx: x, ty: ty - 1 });
    95→    if (isWalkable(x, ty + th)) candidates.push({ tx: x, ty: ty + th });
    96→  }
    97→  for (let y = ty; y < ty + th; y++) {
    98→    if (isWalkable(tx - 1, y)) candidates.push({ tx: tx - 1, ty: y });
    99→    if (isWalkable(tx + tw, y)) candidates.push({ tx: tx + tw, ty: y });
   100→  }
   101→
   102→  if (candidates.length === 0) return null;
   103→  return candidates[Math.floor(Math.random() * candidates.length)];
   104→}
   105→
   106→// ── Character class ──
   107→
   108→export class Character {
   109→  constructor(def) {
   110→    this.id = def.id;
   111→    this.name = def.name;
   112→    this.personality = def.personality;
   113→    this.tx = def.tx;
   114→    this.ty = def.ty;
   115→    this.homeSector = def.sector;
   116→    this.energy = 100;
   117→    this.facing = 'down'; // up | down | left | right
   118→    this.action = 'idle'; // idle | walking | interacting
   119→    this.colors = def.colors; // fallback render colors
   120→
   121→    // Movement
   122→    this.path = [];          // waypoints to follow
   123→    this.moveProgress = 0;   // 0–1 progress toward next waypoint
   124→    this.moveSpeed = 2.0;    // tiles per second
   125→    this.prevTx = def.tx;
   126→    this.prevTy = def.ty;
   127→
   128→    // Walk animation (4-frame cycle: walk1→walk2→walk3→walk4)
   129→    this.walkFrame = 0;      // 0–3 → walk1/walk2/walk3/walk4
   130→    this.walkTimer = 0;
   131→    this.walkFrameMs = 200;  // ms between frame advances
   132→
   133→    // Wander AI
   134→    this.targetObjectId = null;
   135→    this.wanderCooldown = 2000 + Math.random() * 5000; // start soon
   136→    this.interactTimer = 0;
   137→
   138→    // Interaction tracking
   139→    this.lastObjectId = null;
   140→    this.pendingAction = 'use';
   141→    this.targetCharId = null;   // ID of a character to face toward upon arrival
   142→
   143→    // Mood system
   144→    this.mood = 'content';       // happy | content | neutral | upset
   145→    this.moodTimer = 0;          // ms until mood resets to content
   146→  }
   147→
   148→  /** Sprite key for the current animation frame. */
   149→  get spriteKey() {
   150→    if (this.action === 'walking') {
   151→      return `${this.id}_walk${this.walkFrame + 1}`;
   152→    }
   153→    return `${this.id}_idle`;
   154→  }
   155→
   156→  get moodEmoji() {
   157→    switch (this.mood) {
   158→      case 'happy': return '😊';
   159→      case 'content': return '😌';
   160→      case 'upset': return '😤';
   161→      default: return null;
   162→    }
   163→  }
   164→
   165→  /** Pixel X position (interpolated between tiles for smooth movement). */
   166→  get px() {
   167→    if (this.path.length === 0) return this.tx * TILE_SIZE;
   168→    const next = this.path[0];
   169→    return (this.prevTx + (next.tx - this.prevTx) * this.moveProgress) * TILE_SIZE;
   170→  }
   171→
   172→  /** Pixel Y position (interpolated between tiles for smooth movement). */
   173→  get py() {
   174→    if (this.path.length === 0) return this.ty * TILE_SIZE;
   175→    const next = this.path[0];
   176→    return (this.prevTy + (next.ty - this.prevTy) * this.moveProgress) * TILE_SIZE;
   177→  }
   178→
   179→  /** Start moving toward a goal tile. Returns true if a path was found. */
   180→  moveTo(goalTx, goalTy) {
   181→    const path = findPath(Math.round(this.tx), Math.round(this.ty), goalTx, goalTy);
   182→    if (path && path.length > 0) {
   183→      this.path = path;
   184→      this.prevTx = Math.round(this.tx);
   185→      this.prevTy = Math.round(this.ty);
   186→      this.moveProgress = 0;
   187→      this.action = 'walking';
   188→      this._updateFacing(path[0].tx - this.prevTx, path[0].ty - this.prevTy);
   189→      return true;
   190→    }
   191→    return false;
   192→  }
   193→
   194→  /** Per-frame update: advance position and animation. */
   195→  update(deltaMs) {
   196→    if (this.action === 'walking' && this.path.length > 0) {
   197→      // Walk animation cycle (4 frames)
   198→      this.walkTimer += deltaMs;
   199→      if (this.walkTimer >= this.walkFrameMs) {
   200→        this.walkTimer -= this.walkFrameMs;
   201→        this.walkFrame = (this.walkFrame + 1) % 4;
   202→      }
   203→
   204→      // Advance position along path
   205→      const tileDurationMs = 1000 / this.moveSpeed;
   206→      this.moveProgress += deltaMs / tileDurationMs;
   207→
   208→      while (this.moveProgress >= 1 && this.path.length > 0) {
   209→        this.moveProgress -= 1;
   210→        const arrived = this.path.shift();
   211→        this.tx = arrived.tx;
   212→        this.ty = arrived.ty;
   213→        this.prevTx = arrived.tx;
   214→        this.prevTy = arrived.ty;
   215→
   216→        if (this.path.length > 0) {
   217→          this._updateFacing(this.path[0].tx - this.tx, this.path[0].ty - this.ty);
   218→        }
   219→      }
   220→
   221→      // Arrived at destination
   222→      if (this.path.length === 0) {
   223→        this.moveProgress = 0;
   224→        this.action = 'idle';
   225→      }
   226→    } else if (this.action === 'interacting') {
   227→      this.interactTimer -= deltaMs;
   228→      if (this.interactTimer <= 0) {
   229→        this.action = 'idle';
   230→        this.targetObjectId = null;
   231→      }
   232→    }
   233→
   234→    // Decay mood back to content
   235→    if (this.moodTimer > 0) {
   236→      this.moodTimer -= deltaMs;
   237→      if (this.moodTimer <= 0) {
   238→        this.mood = 'content';
   239→        this.moodTimer = 0;
   240→      }
   241→    }
   242→  }
   243→
   244→  _updateFacing(dx, dy) {
   245→    if (dx > 0) this.facing = 'right';
   246→    else if (dx < 0) this.facing = 'left';
   247→    else if (dy > 0) this.facing = 'down';
   248→    else if (dy < 0) this.facing = 'up';
   249→  }
   250→
   251→  /** Enter interaction state (after arriving at an object). */
   252→  startInteraction(objectId) {
   253→    this.action = 'interacting';
   254→    this.targetObjectId = objectId;
   255→    this.lastObjectId = objectId;
   256→    this.interactTimer = 2000;
   257→  }
   258→
   259→  setMood(mood, durationMs = 15000) {
   260→    this.mood = mood;
   261→    this.moodTimer = durationMs;
   262→  }
   263→
   264→  /** Get nearby objects and characters within Manhattan distance radius. */
   265→  getPerception(objects, characters, radius = 5) {
   266→    const nearbyObjects = [];
   267→    const nearbyCharacters = [];
   268→
   269→    for (const obj of objects) {
   270→      const dist = Math.abs(obj.position.tx - this.tx) + Math.abs(obj.position.ty - this.ty);
   271→      if (dist <= radius) {
   272→        nearbyObjects.push({
   273→          id: obj.id,
   274→          name: obj.name,
   275→          state: obj.state,
   276→          distance: dist,
   277→          sector: obj.sector,
   278→        });
   279→      }
   280→    }
   281→
   282→    for (const char of characters) {
   283→      if (char.id === this.id) continue;
   284→      const dist = Math.abs(char.tx - this.tx) + Math.abs(char.ty - this.ty);
   285→      if (dist <= radius) {
   286→        nearbyCharacters.push({
   287→          id: char.id,
   288→          name: char.name,
   289→          action: char.action,
   290→          distance: dist,
   291→        });
   292→      }
   293→    }
   294→
   295→    return { objects: nearbyObjects, characters: nearbyCharacters };
   296→  }
   297→}
   298→
   299→// ── Character definitions ──
   300→
   301→export const CHARACTER_DEFS = [
   302→  {
   303→    id: 'maple',
   304→    name: 'Maple',
   305→    personality: 'Warm, chatty barista who takes pride in her coffee. Runs the café downstairs.',
   306→    sector: 'cafe',
   307→    tx: 3, ty: 12,
   308→    colors: { body: '#e8a87c', hair: '#d4543b', outfit: '#f5e6cc' },
   309→  },
   310→  {
   311→    id: 'sol',
   312→    name: 'Sol',
   313→    personality: 'Quiet, bookish regular who loves the window seat. Friendly when approached.',
   314→    sector: 'cafe',
   315→    tx: 10, ty: 16,
   316→    colors: { body: '#b5835a', hair: '#2d5e2a', outfit: '#7ab648' },
   317→  },
   318→  {
   319→    id: 'nyx',
   320→    name: 'Nyx',
   321→    personality: 'Creative night owl who lives upstairs. Paints, cooks, and is slightly scatterbrained.',
   322→    sector: 'apartment',
   323→    tx: 3, ty: 4,
   324→    colors: { body: '#c4aed0', hair: '#3a2d5e', outfit: '#1a1a2e' },
   325→  },
   326→];
   327→

{
  "content": {
    "tool_use_id": "toolu_01BxFKZhDmnwPNeE9kSzwHD2",
    "type": "tool_result",
    "content": "     1\u2192// \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\n     2\u2192// Character System \u2014 Movement, Perception, A* Pathfinding\n     3\u2192// \u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\u2550\n     4\u2192//\n     5\u2192// Characters have: id, name, personality, position, action state,\n     6\u2192// mood, energy, facing direction. They move between tiles using\n     7\u2192// A* pathfinding on the walkable tilemap.\n     8\u2192//\n     9\u2192// Walk animation alternates walk1/walk2 sprites while moving.\n    10\u2192// Perception system returns nearby objects and characters within radius.\n    11\u2192\n    12\u2192import { MAP, T, MAP_COLS, MAP_ROWS, TILE_SIZE } from './tilemap.js';\n    13\u2192\n    14\u2192// \u2500\u2500 Walkable tiles (everything except walls, fences, water) \u2500\u2500\n    15\u2192const UNWALKABLE = new Set([T.WALL, T.WALL_TOP, T.COUNTER, T.COUNTER_L, T.COUNTER_R, T.WINDOW]);\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192export function isWalkable(tx, ty) {\n    18\u2192  if (tx < 0 || tx >= MAP_COLS || ty < 0 || ty >= MAP_ROWS) return false;\n    19\u2192  return !UNWALKABLE.has(MAP[ty][tx]);\n    20\u2192}\n    21\u2192\n    22\u2192// \u2500\u2500 A* Pathfinding \u2500\u2500\n    23\u2192// Returns array of {tx, ty} waypoints (excludes start, includes goal),\n    24\u2192// or null if no path found. Returns [] if already at goal.\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192const NEIGHBORS = [[0, -1], [0, 1], [-1, 0], [1, 0]];\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192export function findPath(sx, sy, gx, gy) {\n    29\u2192  if (!isWalkable(gx, gy)) return null;\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192  const startKey = sy * MAP_COLS + sx;\n    32\u2192  const goalKey = gy * MAP_COLS + gx;\n    33\u2192  if (startKey === goalKey) return [];\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192  const open = [startKey];\n    36\u2192  const cameFrom = new Map();\n    37\u2192  const gScore = new Map([[startKey, 0]]);\n    38\u2192  const fScore = new Map([[startKey, Math.abs(sx - gx) + Math.abs(sy - gy)]]);\n    39\u2192  const closed = new Set();\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192  while (open.length > 0) {\n    42\u2192    // Find node with lowest fScore\n    43\u2192    let bestIdx = 0;\n    44\u2192    let bestF = fScore.get(open[0]) ?? Infinity;\n    45\u2192    for (let i = 1; i < open.length; i++) {\n    46\u2192      const f = fScore.get(open[i]) ?? Infinity;\n    47\u2192      if (f < bestF) { bestF = f; bestIdx = i; }\n    48\u2192    }\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192    const current = open[bestIdx];\n    51\u2192    if (current === goalKey) {\n    52\u2192      const path = [];\n    53\u2192      let c = current;\n    54\u2192      while (cameFrom.has(c)) {\n    55\u2192        path.unshift({ tx: c % MAP_COLS, ty: Math.floor(c / MAP_COLS) });\n    56\u2192        c = cameFrom.get(c);\n    57\u2192      }\n    58\u2192      return path;\n    59\u2192    }\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192    open.splice(bestIdx, 1);\n    62\u2192    closed.add(current);\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192    const cx = current % MAP_COLS;\n    65\u2192    const cy = Math.floor(current / MAP_COLS);\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192    for (const [dx, dy] of NEIGHBORS) {\n    68\u2192      const nx = cx + dx;\n    69\u2192      const ny = cy + dy;\n    70\u2192      if (!isWalkable(nx, ny)) continue;\n    71\u2192\n    72\u2192      const nKey = ny * MAP_COLS + nx;\n    73\u2192      if (closed.has(nKey)) continue;\n    74\u2192\n    75\u2192      const tentG = (gScore.get(current) ?? Infinity) + 1;\n    76\u2192      if (tentG < (gScore.get(nKey) ?? Infinity)) {\n    77\u2192        cameFrom.set(nKey, current);\n    78\u2192        gScore.set(nKey, tentG);\n    79\u2192        fScore.set(nKey, tentG + Math.abs(nx - gx) + Math.abs(ny - gy));\n    80\u2192        if (!open.includes(nKey)) open.push(nKey);\n    81\u2192      }\n    82\u2192    }\n    83\u2192  }\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192  return null; // no path\n    86\u2192}\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192// \u2500\u2500 Find walkable tile adjacent to an object (for interaction) \u2500\u2500\n    89\u2192export function findInteractTile(objPosition) {\n    90\u2192  const { tx, ty, tw = 1, th = 1 } = objPosition;\n    91\u2192  const candidates = [];\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192  for (let x = tx - 1; x <= tx + tw; x++) {\n    94\u2192    if (isWalkable(x, ty - 1)) candidates.push({ tx: x, ty: ty - 1 });\n    95\u2192    if (isWalkable(x, ty + th)) candidates.push({ tx: x, ty: ty + th });\n    96\u2192  }\n    97\u2192  for (let y = ty; y < ty + th; y++) {\n    98\u2192    if (isWalkable(tx - 1, y)) candidates.push({ tx: tx - 1, ty: y });\n    99\u2192    if (isWalkable(tx + tw, y)) candidates.push({ tx: tx + tw, ty: y });\n   100\u2192  }\n   101\u2192\n   102\u2192  if (candidates.length === 0) return null;\n   103\u2192  return candidates[Math.floor(Math.random() * candidates.length)];\n   104\u2192}\n   105\u2192\n   106\u2192// \u2500\u2500 Character class \u2500\u2500\n   107\u2192\n   108\u2192export class Character {\n   109\u2192  constructor(def) {\n   110\u2192    this.id = def.id;\n   111\u2192    this.name = def.name;\n   112\u2192    this.personality = def.personality;\n   113\u2192    this.tx = def.tx;\n   114\u2192    this.ty = def.ty;\n   115\u2192    this.homeSector = def.sector;\n   116\u2192    this.energy = 100;\n   117\u2192    this.facing = 'down'; // up | down | left | right\n   118\u2192    this.action = 'idle'; // idle | walking | interacting\n   119\u2192    this.colors = def.colors; // fallback render colors\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192    // Movement\n   122\u2192    this.path = [];          // waypoints to follow\n   123\u2192    this.moveProgress = 0;   // 0\u20131 progress toward next waypoint\n   124\u2192    this.moveSpeed = 2.0;    // tiles per second\n   125\u2192    this.prevTx = def.tx;\n   126\u2192    this.prevTy = def.ty;\n   127\u2192\n   128\u2192    // Walk animation (4-frame cycle: walk1\u2192walk2\u2192walk3\u2192walk4)\n   129\u2192    this.walkFrame = 0;      // 0\u20133 \u2192 walk1/walk2/walk3/walk4\n   130\u2192    this.walkTimer = 0;\n   131\u2192    this.walkFrameMs = 200;  // ms between frame advances\n   132\u2192\n   133\u2192    // Wander AI\n   134\u2192    this.targetObjectId = null;\n   135\u2192    this.wanderCooldown = 2000 + Math.random() * 5000; // start soon\n   136\u2192    this.interactTimer = 0;\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192    // Interaction tracking\n   139\u2192    this.lastObjectId = null;\n   140\u2192    this.pendingAction = 'use';\n   141\u2192    this.targetCharId = null;   // ID of a character to face toward upon arrival\n   142\u2192\n   143\u2192    // Mood system\n   144\u2192    this.mood = 'content';       // happy | content | neutral | upset\n   145\u2192    this.moodTimer = 0;          // ms until mood resets to content\n   146\u2192  }\n   147\u2192\n   148\u2192  /** Sprite key for the current animation frame. */\n   149\u2192  get spriteKey() {\n   150\u2192    if (this.action === 'walking') {\n   151\u2192      return `${this.id}_walk${this.walkFrame + 1}`;\n   152\u2192    }\n   153\u2192    return `${this.id}_idle`;\n   154\u2192  }\n   155\u2192\n   156\u2192  get moodEmoji() {\n   157\u2192    switch (this.mood) {\n   158\u2192      case 'happy': return '\ud83d\ude0a';\n   159\u2192      case 'content': return '\ud83d\ude0c';\n   160\u2192      case 'upset': return '\ud83d\ude24';\n   161\u2192      default: return null;\n   162\u2192    }\n   163\u2192  }\n   164\u2192\n   165\u2192  /** Pixel X position (interpolated between tiles for smooth movement). */\n   166\u2192  get px() {\n   167\u2192    if (this.path.length === 0) return this.tx * TILE_SIZE;\n   168\u2192    const next = this.path[0];\n   169\u2192    return (this.prevTx + (next.tx - this.prevTx) * this.moveProgress) * TILE_SIZE;\n   170\u2192  }\n   171\u2192\n   172\u2192  /** Pixel Y position (interpolated between tiles for smooth movement). */\n   173\u2192  get py() {\n   174\u2192    if (this.path.length === 0) return this.ty * TILE_SIZE;\n   175\u2192    const next = this.path[0];\n   176\u2192    return (this.prevTy + (next.ty - this.prevTy) * this.moveProgress) * TILE_SIZE;\n   177\u2192  }\n   178\u2192\n   179\u2192  /** Start moving toward a goal tile. Returns true if a path was found. */\n   180\u2192  moveTo(goalTx, goalTy) {\n   181\u2192    const path = findPath(Math.round(this.tx), Math.round(this.ty), goalTx, goalTy);\n   182\u2192    if (path && path.length > 0) {\n   183\u2192      this.path = path;\n   184\u2192      this.prevTx = Math.round(this.tx);\n   185\u2192      this.prevTy = Math.round(this.ty);\n   186\u2192      this.moveProgress = 0;\n   187\u2192      this.action = 'walking';\n   188\u2192      this._updateFacing(path[0].tx - this.prevTx, path[0].ty - this.prevTy);\n   189\u2192      return true;\n   190\u2192    }\n   191\u2192    return false;\n   192\u2192  }\n   193\u2192\n   194\u2192  /** Per-frame update: advance position and animation. */\n   195\u2192  update(deltaMs) {\n   196\u2192    if (this.action === 'walking' && this.path.length > 0) {\n   197\u2192      // Walk animation cycle (4 frames)\n   198\u2192      this.walkTimer += deltaMs;\n   199\u2192      if (this.walkTimer >= this.walkFrameMs) {\n   200\u2192        this.walkTimer -= this.walkFrameMs;\n   201\u2192        this.walkFrame = (this.walkFrame + 1) % 4;\n   202\u2192      }\n   203\u2192\n   204\u2192      // Advance position along path\n   205\u2192      const tileDurationMs = 1000 / this.moveSpeed;\n   206\u2192      this.moveProgress += deltaMs / tileDurationMs;\n   207\u2192\n   208\u2192      while (this.moveProgress >= 1 && this.path.length > 0) {\n   209\u2192        this.moveProgress -= 1;\n   210\u2192        const arrived = this.path.shift();\n   211\u2192        this.tx = arrived.tx;\n   212\u2192        this.ty = arrived.ty;\n   213\u2192        this.prevTx = arrived.tx;\n   214\u2192        this.prevTy = arrived.ty;\n   215\u2192\n   216\u2192        if (this.path.length > 0) {\n   217\u2192          this._updateFacing(this.path[0].tx - this.tx, this.path[0].ty - this.ty);\n   218\u2192        }\n   219\u2192      }\n   220\u2192\n   221\u2192      // Arrived at destination\n   222\u2192      if (this.path.length === 0) {\n   223\u2192        this.moveProgress = 0;\n   224\u2192        this.action = 'idle';\n   225\u2192      }\n   226\u2192    } else if (this.action === 'interacting') {\n   227\u2192      this.interactTimer -= deltaMs;\n   228\u2192      if (this.interactTimer <= 0) {\n   229\u2192        this.action = 'idle';\n   230\u2192        this.targetObjectId = null;\n   231\u2192      }\n   232\u2192    }\n   233\u2192\n   234\u2192    // Decay mood back to content\n   235\u2192    if (this.moodTimer > 0) {\n   236\u2192      this.moodTimer -= deltaMs;\n   237\u2192      if (this.moodTimer <= 0) {\n   238\u2192        this.mood = 'content';\n   239\u2192        this.moodTimer = 0;\n   240\u2192      }\n   241\u2192    }\n   242\u2192  }\n   243\u2192\n   244\u2192  _updateFacing(dx, dy) {\n   245\u2192    if (dx > 0) this.facing = 'right';\n   246\u2192    else if (dx < 0) this.facing = 'left';\n   247\u2192    else if (dy > 0) this.facing = 'down';\n   248\u2192    else if (dy < 0) this.facing = 'up';\n   249\u2192  }\n   250\u2192\n   251\u2192  /** Enter interaction state (after arriving at an object). */\n   252\u2192  startInteraction(objectId) {\n   253\u2192    this.action = 'interacting';\n   254\u2192    this.targetObjectId = objectId;\n   255\u2192    this.lastObjectId = objectId;\n   256\u2192    this.interactTimer = 2000;\n   257\u2192  }\n   258\u2192\n   259\u2192  setMood(mood, durationMs = 15000) {\n   260\u2192    this.mood = mood;\n   261\u2192    this.moodTimer = durationMs;\n   262\u2192  }\n   263\u2192\n   264\u2192  /** Get nearby objects and characters within Manhattan distance radius. */\n   265\u2192  getPerception(objects, characters, radius = 5) {\n   266\u2192    const nearbyObjects = [];\n   267\u2192    const nearbyCharacters = [];\n   268\u2192\n   269\u2192    for (const obj of objects) {\n   270\u2192      const dist = Math.abs(obj.position.tx - this.tx) + Math.abs(obj.position.ty - this.ty);\n   271\u2192      if (dist <= radius) {\n   272\u2192        nearbyObjects.push({\n   273\u2192          id: obj.id,\n   274\u2192          name: obj.name,\n   275\u2192          state: obj.state,\n   276\u2192          distance: dist,\n   277\u2192          sector: obj.sector,\n   278\u2192        });\n   279\u2192      }\n   280\u2192    }\n   281\u2192\n   282\u2192    for (const char of characters) {\n   283\u2192      if (char.id === this.id) continue;\n   284\u2192      const dist = Math.abs(char.tx - this.tx) + Math.abs(char.ty - this.ty);\n   285\u2192      if (dist <= radius) {\n   286\u2192        nearbyCharacters.push({\n   287\u2192          id: char.id,\n   288\u2192          name: char.name,\n   289\u2192          action: char.action,\n   290\u2192          distance: dist,\n   291\u2192        });\n   292\u2192      }\n   293\u2192    }\n   294\u2192\n   295\u2192    return { objects: nearbyObjects, characters: nearbyCharacters };\n   296\u2192  }\n   297\u2192}\n   298\u2192\n   299\u2192// \u2500\u2500 Character definitions \u2500\u2500\n   300\u2192\n   301\u2192export const CHARACTER_DEFS = [\n   302\u2192  {\n   303\u2192    id: 'maple',\n   304\u2192    name: 'Maple',\n   305\u2192    personality: 'Warm, chatty barista who takes pride in her coffee. Runs the caf\u00e9 downstairs.',\n   306\u2192    sector: 'cafe',\n   307\u2192    tx: 3, ty: 12,\n   308\u2192    colors: { body: '#e8a87c', hair: '#d4543b', outfit: '#f5e6cc' },\n   309\u2192  },\n   310\u2192  {\n   311\u2192    id: 'sol',\n   312\u2192    name: 'Sol',\n   313\u2192    personality: 'Quiet, bookish regular who loves the window seat. Friendly when approached.',\n   314\u2192    sector: 'cafe',\n   315\u2192    tx: 10, ty: 16,\n   316\u2192    colors: { body: '#b5835a', hair: '#2d5e2a', outfit: '#7ab648' },\n   317\u2192  },\n   318\u2192  {\n   319\u2192    id: 'nyx',\n   320\u2192    name: 'Nyx',\n   321\u2192    personality: 'Creative night owl who lives upstairs. Paints, cooks, and is slightly scatterbrained.',\n   322\u2192    sector: 'apartment',\n   323\u2192    tx: 3, ty: 4,\n   324\u2192    colors: { body: '#c4aed0', hair: '#3a2d5e', outfit: '#1a1a2e' },\n   325\u2192  },\n   326\u2192];\n   327\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
  }
}
