USERMESSAGE #66
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     2→// API Client — Calls the Cloudflare Worker Grok proxy
     3→// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     4→//
     5→// Queues requests to avoid overwhelming the worker.
     6→// Returns fallback actions if the API is unavailable.
     7→
     8→const DEFAULT_ENDPOINT = 'http://localhost:8787';
     9→
    10→// ── Fallback actions when API is unreachable ──
    11→
    12→const FALLBACK_ACTIONS = [
    13→  { action: 'move', target: 'crossroads', description: 'Wanders to the crossroads', dialogue: null },
    14→  { action: 'interact', target: null, description: 'Looks around quietly', dialogue: null },
    15→  { action: 'move', target: 'garden', description: 'Strolls toward the garden', dialogue: null },
    16→];
    17→
    18→function getRandomFallback() {
    19→  return { ...FALLBACK_ACTIONS[Math.floor(Math.random() * FALLBACK_ACTIONS.length)] };
    20→}
    21→
    22→// ── Request queue ──
    23→
    24→export class AIClient {
    25→  constructor(options = {}) {
    26→    this.endpoint = options.endpoint || DEFAULT_ENDPOINT;
    27→    this.maxConcurrent = options.maxConcurrent || 2;
    28→    this.timeoutMs = options.timeoutMs || 10_000;
    29→
    30→    this._active = 0;
    31→    this._queue = []; // { resolve, reject, payload }
    32→  }
    33→
    34→  /**
    35→   * Request a character decision from the AI.
    36→   * @param {Object} params
    37→   * @param {string} params.character — character name
    38→   * @param {string} params.personality — personality description
    39→   * @param {Object} params.perception — { objects: [...], characters: [...] }
    40→   * @param {string} params.mood — current mood
    41→   * @param {number} params.energy — 0-100
    42→   * @param {string} params.time — time of day string
    43→   * @returns {Promise<{action, target, description, dialogue}>}
    44→   */
    45→  async decide(params) {
    46→    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
    47→      this._queue.push({ resolve, reject, payload: params });
    48→      this._drain();
    49→    });
    50→  }
    51→
    52→  _drain() {
    53→    while (this._active < this.maxConcurrent && this._queue.length > 0) {
    54→      const job = this._queue.shift();
    55→      this._active++;
    56→      this._send(job.payload)
    57→        .then(job.resolve)
    58→        .catch(job.reject)
    59→        .finally(() => {
    60→          this._active--;
    61→          this._drain();
    62→        });
    63→    }
    64→  }
    65→
    66→  async _send(payload) {
    67→    const controller = new AbortController();
    68→    const timer = setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), this.timeoutMs);
    69→
    70→    try {
    71→      const res = await fetch(this.endpoint, {
    72→        method: 'POST',
    73→        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    74→        body: JSON.stringify(payload),
    75→        signal: controller.signal,
    76→      });
    77→
    78→      if (!res.ok) {
    79→        console.warn(`AI API error ${res.status}`);
    80→        return getRandomFallback();
    81→      }
    82→
    83→      const data = await res.json();
    84→
    85→      // Validate response shape
    86→      if (!data.action) {
    87→        console.warn('AI API returned invalid response:', data);
    88→        return getRandomFallback();
    89→      }
    90→
    91→      return data;
    92→    } catch (err) {
    93→      if (err.name === 'AbortError') {
    94→        console.warn('AI API request timed out');
    95→      } else {
    96→        console.warn('AI API request failed:', err.message);
    97→      }
    98→      return getRandomFallback();
    99→    } finally {
   100→      clearTimeout(timer);
   101→    }
   102→  }
   103→}
   104→

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

     1→<!DOCTYPE html>
     2→<html lang="en">
     3→<head>
     4→  <meta charset="utf-8">
     5→  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">
     6→  <title>AI Aquarium</title>
     7→  <style>
     8→    * { margin: 0; padding: 0; box-sizing: border-box; }
     9→    body {
    10→      background: #0a0a14;
    11→      overflow: hidden;
    12→      display: flex;
    13→      justify-content: center;
    14→      align-items: center;
    15→      width: 100vw;
    16→      height: 100vh;
    17→    }
    18→
    19→    #canvas-container {
    20→      position: relative;
    21→    }
    22→
    23→    /* Debug overlay */
    24→    #debug-overlay {
    25→      position: fixed;
    26→      top: 0;
    27→      left: 0;
    28→      right: 0;
    29→      background: rgba(10, 10, 20, 0.92);
    30→      color: #e0ddd4;
    31→      font-family: 'Courier New', monospace;
    32→      font-size: 11px;
    33→      padding: 8px 12px;
    34→      z-index: 10;
    35→      display: none;
    36→      max-height: 50vh;
    37→      overflow-y: auto;
    38→    }
    39→    #debug-overlay.visible { display: block; }
    40→
    41→    #debug-overlay h2 {
    42→      font-size: 12px;
    43→      color: #FFD700;
    44→      margin-bottom: 4px;
    45→    }
    46→
    47→    .debug-controls {
    48→      display: flex;
    49→      gap: 6px;
    50→      align-items: center;
    51→      margin-bottom: 6px;
    52→      flex-wrap: wrap;
    53→    }
    54→    .debug-controls button {
    55→      background: #2a2a4a;
    56→      color: #e0ddd4;
    57→      border: 1px solid #444;
    58→      padding: 3px 10px;
    59→      border-radius: 3px;
    60→      cursor: pointer;
    61→      font-family: inherit;
    62→      font-size: 11px;
    63→    }
    64→    .debug-controls button:hover { background: #3a3a5a; }
    65→    .debug-controls button.active { background: #4a6a3a; border-color: #6a8a5a; }
    66→
    67→    .debug-status {
    68→      display: flex;
    69→      gap: 16px;
    70→      margin-bottom: 6px;
    71→      color: #aaa;
    72→      font-size: 10px;
    73→    }
    74→    .debug-status .running { color: #7c7; }
    75→    .debug-status .paused { color: #cc7; }
    76→    .debug-status .stopped { color: #c77; }
    77→
    78→    .debug-log {
    79→      max-height: 100px;
    80→      overflow-y: auto;
    81→      border-top: 1px solid #333;
    82→      padding-top: 4px;
    83→      margin-top: 4px;
    84→    }
    85→    .debug-log .entry { padding: 1px 0; }
    86→    .debug-log .obj-name { color: #8ac; }
    87→    .debug-log .from { color: #c88; }
    88→    .debug-log .to { color: #8c8; }
    89→    .debug-log .trigger { color: #cc8; }
    90→
    91→    .debug-chars {
    92→      border-top: 1px solid #333;
    93→      padding-top: 4px;
    94→      margin-top: 4px;
    95→    }
    96→    .debug-chars .char-label { color: #c8a; }
    97→    .debug-chars .char-action { color: #8ac; }
    98→    .debug-chars .char-target { color: #888; }
    99→
   100→    .debug-api {
   101→      margin-top: 4px;
   102→      padding-top: 4px;
   103→      border-top: 1px solid #333;
   104→      display: flex;
   105→      align-items: center;
   106→      gap: 6px;
   107→      flex-wrap: wrap;
   108→    }
   109→    .debug-api input {
   110→      background: #1a1a2e;
   111→      color: #e0ddd4;
   112→      border: 1px solid #444;
   113→      padding: 2px 6px;
   114→      border-radius: 3px;
   115→      font-family: inherit;
   116→      font-size: 10px;
   117→      width: 200px;
   118→    }
   119→    .debug-api input:focus { border-color: #6a8a5a; outline: none; }
   120→    .debug-api .api-warn { color: #c77; font-size: 10px; }
   121→
   122→    /* Click tooltip */
   123→    #click-tooltip {
   124→      position: fixed;
   125→      background: rgba(20, 20, 40, 0.95);
   126→      color: #e0ddd4;
   127→      font-family: 'Courier New', monospace;
   128→      font-size: 11px;
   129→      padding: 6px 10px;
   130→      border-radius: 4px;
   131→      border: 1px solid #555;
   132→      pointer-events: none;
   133→      z-index: 20;
   134→      display: none;
   135→    }
   136→
   137→    /* Hint text */
   138→    #hint {
   139→      position: fixed;
   140→      bottom: 8px;
   141→      right: 12px;
   142→      color: #444;
   143→      font-family: 'Courier New', monospace;
   144→      font-size: 10px;
   145→      z-index: 5;
   146→    }
   147→
   148→    /* Click-to-start overlay */
   149→    #start-overlay {
   150→      position: fixed;
   151→      inset: 0;
   152→      background: rgba(6, 6, 16, 0.92);
   153→      display: flex;
   154→      flex-direction: column;
   155→      justify-content: center;
   156→      align-items: center;
   157→      z-index: 100;
   158→      cursor: pointer;
   159→      user-select: none;
   160→    }
   161→    #start-overlay .title {
   162→      font-family: 'Courier New', monospace;
   163→      font-size: 36px;
   164→      color: #e0ddd4;
   165→      letter-spacing: 4px;
   166→      text-transform: uppercase;
   167→      margin-bottom: 24px;
   168→      text-shadow: 0 0 20px rgba(100, 140, 200, 0.3);
   169→    }
   170→    #start-overlay .subtitle {
   171→      font-family: 'Courier New', monospace;
   172→      font-size: 14px;
   173→      color: #8888aa;
   174→      animation: pulse 2.5s ease-in-out infinite;
   175→    }
   176→    @keyframes pulse {
   177→      0%, 100% { opacity: 0.5; }
   178→      50% { opacity: 1; }
   179→    }
   180→    #start-overlay.hidden {
   181→      display: none;
   182→    }
   183→
   184→    /* Mute toggle button */
   185→    #mute-btn {
   186→      position: fixed;
   187→      top: 10px;
   188→      right: 10px;
   189→      z-index: 50;
   190→      background: rgba(20, 20, 40, 0.7);
   191→      border: 1px solid #444;
   192→      border-radius: 4px;
   193→      color: #e0ddd4;
   194→      font-size: 18px;
   195→      width: 32px;
   196→      height: 32px;
   197→      cursor: pointer;
   198→      display: none;
   199→      align-items: center;
   200→      justify-content: center;
   201→      line-height: 1;
   202→      padding: 0;
   203→    }
   204→    #mute-btn:hover { background: rgba(40, 40, 70, 0.8); }
   205→    #mute-btn.visible { display: flex; }
   206→  </style>
   207→</head>
   208→<body>
   209→
   210→<div id="start-overlay">
   211→  <div class="title">AI Aquarium</div>
   212→  <div class="subtitle">Click anywhere to start</div>
   213→</div>
   214→
   215→<button id="mute-btn" aria-label="Toggle audio">🔊</button>
   216→
   217→<div id="canvas-container"></div>
   218→
   219→<div id="debug-overlay">
   220→  <h2>AI Aquarium Debug <span id="debugToggleHint" style="color:#666; font-weight:normal">[D to toggle]</span></h2>
   221→  <div class="debug-controls">
   222→    <button id="startBtn">Start</button>
   223→    <button id="pauseBtn">Pause</button>
   224→    <button id="stopBtn">Stop</button>
   225→    <button id="tickBtn">+1 Tick</button>
   226→    <button id="tick10Btn">+10</button>
   227→    <span style="color:#666">|</span>
   228→    <button id="speed1Btn" class="active">1x</button>
   229→    <button id="speed5Btn">5x</button>
   230→    <button id="speed20Btn">20x</button>
   231→    <span style="color:#666">|</span>
   232→    <button id="aiToggleBtn">AI: Off</button>
   233→    <span style="color:#666">|</span>
   234→    <button id="resetBtn">Reset</button>
   235→  </div>
   236→  <div class="debug-api">
   237→    <label style="color:#888; font-size:10px">API:</label>
   238→    <input id="apiEndpoint" type="text" placeholder="http://localhost:8787">
   239→    <span id="apiWarning" class="api-warn">&#9888; No endpoint set</span>
   240→  </div>
   241→  <div class="debug-status" id="debugStatus">
   242→    <span>State: <span id="engineState" class="stopped">stopped</span></span>
   243→    <span>Tick: <span id="tickCount">0</span></span>
   244→    <span>Elapsed: <span id="elapsedTime">0s</span></span>
   245→    <span>Speed: <span id="timeScale">1x</span></span>
   246→    <span>Clock: <span id="simClockDisplay">Day 1 8:00 AM</span></span>
   247→    <span>AI: <span id="aiModeDisplay" style="color:#c77">off</span></span>
   248→  </div>
   249→  <div class="debug-chars" id="charStatus"></div>
   250→  <div class="debug-log" id="debugLog"></div>
   251→</div>
   252→
   253→<div id="click-tooltip"></div>
   254→<div id="hint">Press D for debug</div>
   255→
   256→<script type="module">
   257→import { SimulationEngine } from './src/engine.js';
   258→import { Renderer, preloadSprites } from './src/renderer.js';
   259→import { TILE_SIZE } from './src/tilemap.js';
   260→import { WORLD_TREE } from './src/world.js';
   261→import { AIDecisionEngine } from './src/ai.js';
   262→import { AudioEngine } from './src/audio.js';
   263→
   264→// ── AI setup ──
   265→const aiEngine = new AIDecisionEngine();
   266→
   267→// ── Audio setup ──
   268→const audio = new AudioEngine();
   269→// Restore mute preference
   270→const savedMute = localStorage.getItem('ai-aquarium-muted');
   271→if (savedMute === 'true') {
   272→  audio.muted = true;
   273→}
   274→
   275→// ── State ──
   276→let engine = new SimulationEngine({ aiEngine });
   277→let debugVisible = false;
   278→const eventLog = [];
   279→const MAX_LOG = 30;
   280→
   281→// ── Renderer setup ──
   282→const container = document.getElementById('canvas-container');
   283→const renderer = new Renderer(container);
   284→
   285→// ── DOM refs ──
   286→const $ = (id) => document.getElementById(id);
   287→const debugOverlay = $('debug-overlay');
   288→const tooltip = $('click-tooltip');
   289→
   290→// ── Debug toggle ──
   291→document.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {
   292→  if (e.key === 'd' || e.key === 'D') {
   293→    debugVisible = !debugVisible;
   294→    debugOverlay.classList.toggle('visible', debugVisible);
   295→  }
   296→});
   297→
   298→// ── Engine event listeners ──
   299→function attachEngineListeners() {
   300→  engine.on('object:transition', (event) => {
   301→    eventLog.unshift(event);
   302→    if (eventLog.length > MAX_LOG) eventLog.pop();
   303→    renderDebugLog();
   304→
   305→    // Trigger emoji float on the renderer
   306→    if (event.emoji) {
   307→      const obj = engine.getObject(event.objectId);
   308→      if (obj) {
   309→        renderer.addEmojiFloat(obj.position.tx, obj.position.ty, event.emoji);
   310→      }
   311→    }
   312→  });
   313→
   314→  engine.on('character:interact', (event) => {
   315→    eventLog.unshift({
   316→      objectName: event.objectName,
   317→      from: event.from,
   318→      to: event.to,
   319→      trigger: event.characterName,
   320→      emoji: event.emoji || null,
   321→    });
   322→    if (eventLog.length > MAX_LOG) eventLog.pop();
   323→    renderDebugLog();
   324→
   325→    // Emoji float on character for production events
   326→    if (event.produces) {
   327→      const char = engine.getCharacters().find(c => c.id === event.characterId);
   328→      if (char) {
   329→        const emoji = event.produces === 'bread' ? '🍞' :
   330→                      event.produces === 'burnt_bread' ? '💨' : '✨';
   331→        renderer.addEmojiFloat(Math.round(char.tx), Math.round(char.ty), emoji);
   332→      }
   333→    }
   334→  });
   335→
   336→  engine.on('engine:tick', () => {
   337→    // Update status bar with sim clock + last event
   338→    const clock = engine.simClock;
   339→    const status = engine.lastStatusText || '';
   340→    const line = status
   341→      ? `${clock.dayTimeString} \u2014 ${status}`
   342→      : clock.dayTimeString;
   343→    renderer.setStatus(line);
   344→
   345→    // Sync audio ambient layers with time of day
   346→    audio.setTimeOfDay(clock.minuteOfDay);
   347→
   348→    renderDebugStatus();
   349→  });
   350→
   351→  engine.on('ai:speech', (event) => {
   352→    const char = engine.getCharacters().find(c => c.id === event.characterId);
   353→    if (char) {
   354→      renderer.addCharacterSpeech(char, event.dialogue, 5000);
   355→    }
   356→  });
   357→
   358→  engine.on('ai:status', (event) => {
   359→    engine.lastStatusText = `${event.characterName} ${event.description}`;
   360→  });
   361→}
   362→attachEngineListeners();
   363→
   364→// ── Debug controls ──
   365→$('startBtn').onclick = () => { engine.start(); renderDebugStatus(); };
   366→$('pauseBtn').onclick = () => {
   367→  if (engine.paused) engine.resume(); else engine.pause();
   368→  renderDebugStatus();
   369→};
   370→$('stopBtn').onclick = () => { engine.stop(); renderDebugStatus(); };
   371→$('tickBtn').onclick = () => { engine.manualTick(1); renderDebugStatus(); };
   372→$('tick10Btn').onclick = () => { engine.manualTick(10); renderDebugStatus(); };
   373→
   374→function setSpeed(scale) {
   375→  engine.timeScale = scale;
   376→  $('speed1Btn').classList.toggle('active', scale === 1);
   377→  $('speed5Btn').classList.toggle('active', scale === 5);
   378→  $('speed20Btn').classList.toggle('active', scale === 20);
   379→  renderDebugStatus();
   380→}
   381→$('speed1Btn').onclick = () => setSpeed(1);
   382→$('speed5Btn').onclick = () => setSpeed(5);
   383→$('speed20Btn').onclick = () => setSpeed(20);
   384→
   385→// ── AI controls ──
   386→const apiInput = $('apiEndpoint');
   387→const apiWarning = $('apiWarning');
   388→
   389→apiInput.addEventListener('change', () => {
   390→  const url = apiInput.value.trim();
   391→  aiEngine.setEndpoint(url);
   392→  apiWarning.style.display = url ? 'none' : 'inline';
   393→  if (engine.aiMode && !url) {
   394→    apiWarning.textContent = '\u26A0 AI on but no endpoint \u2014 will wander randomly';
   395→    apiWarning.style.display = 'inline';
   396→  }
   397→});
   398→
   399→$('aiToggleBtn').onclick = () => {
   400→  engine.aiMode = !engine.aiMode;
   401→  const btn = $('aiToggleBtn');
   402→  btn.textContent = `AI: ${engine.aiMode ? 'On' : 'Off'}`;
   403→  btn.classList.toggle('active', engine.aiMode);
   404→
   405→  const url = apiInput.value.trim();
   406→  if (engine.aiMode && !url) {
   407→    apiWarning.textContent = '\u26A0 AI on but no endpoint \u2014 will wander randomly';
   408→    apiWarning.style.display = 'inline';
   409→  } else if (url) {
   410→    apiWarning.style.display = 'none';
   411→  }
   412→  renderDebugStatus();
   413→};
   414→
   415→$('resetBtn').onclick = () => {
   416→  engine.stop();
   417→  engine = new SimulationEngine({ aiEngine });
   418→  // Preserve AI mode state across reset
   419→  const aiOn = $('aiToggleBtn').textContent.includes('On');
   420→  engine.aiMode = aiOn;
   421→  eventLog.length = 0;
   422→  attachEngineListeners();
   423→  renderDebugStatus();
   424→  renderDebugLog();
   425→  renderer.setStatus('');
   426→};
   427→
   428→// ── Debug status render ──
   429→function renderDebugStatus() {
   430→  const stateEl = $('engineState');
   431→  if (engine.running && !engine.paused) {
   432→    stateEl.textContent = 'running';
   433→    stateEl.className = 'running';
   434→  } else if (engine.paused) {
   435→    stateEl.textContent = 'paused';
   436→    stateEl.className = 'paused';
   437→  } else {
   438→    stateEl.textContent = 'stopped';
   439→    stateEl.className = 'stopped';
   440→  }
   441→  $('tickCount').textContent = engine.tickCount;
   442→  $('elapsedTime').textContent = formatMs(engine.elapsedMs);
   443→  $('timeScale').textContent = engine.timeScale + 'x';
   444→
   445→  $('simClockDisplay').textContent = engine.simClock.dayTimeString;
   446→  const aiDisplay = $('aiModeDisplay');
   447→  aiDisplay.textContent = engine.aiMode ? 'on' : 'off';
   448→  aiDisplay.style.color = engine.aiMode ? '#7c7' : '#c77';
   449→
   450→  renderCharStatus();
   451→}
   452→
   453→function renderDebugLog() {
   454→  const el = $('debugLog');
   455→  if (eventLog.length === 0) {
   456→    el.innerHTML = '<div style="color:#555">No events yet</div>';
   457→    return;
   458→  }
   459→  let html = '';
   460→  for (const e of eventLog) {
   461→    const emoji = e.emoji || '';
   462→    html += `<div class="entry">${emoji} <span class="obj-name">${e.objectName}</span>: <span class="from">${e.from}</span> → <span class="to">${e.to}</span> <span class="trigger">[${e.trigger}]</span></div>`;
   463→  }
   464→  el.innerHTML = html;
   465→}
   466→
   467→function renderCharStatus() {
   468→  const chars = engine.getCharacters();
   469→  let html = '<div style="color:#cc8; font-size:10px; margin-bottom:2px">Characters:</div>';
   470→  for (const c of chars) {
   471→    const target = c.targetObjectId ? ` <span class="char-target">→ ${c.targetObjectId}</span>` : '';
   472→    const moodIcon = c.moodEmoji || '';
   473→    const lastObj = c.lastObjectId ? ` <span style="color:#555">last:${c.lastObjectId}</span>` : '';
   474→    html += `<div style="padding:1px 0"><span class="char-label">${c.name}</span> ${moodIcon}: ` +
   475→      `<span class="char-action">${c.action}</span> ` +
   476→      `<span style="color:#686">[${c.mood}]</span> ` +
   477→      `(${Math.round(c.tx)},${Math.round(c.ty)})${target}${lastObj}</div>`;
   478→  }
   479→  $('charStatus').innerHTML = html;
   480→}
   481→
   482→// ── Canvas click → find object at tile → trigger interaction ──
   483→renderer.canvas.addEventListener('click', (e) => {
   484→  const { tx, ty } = renderer.pageToTile(e.clientX, e.clientY);
   485→  const obj = findObjectAtTile(tx, ty);
   486→
   487→  if (obj) {
   488→    const stateDef = obj.currentStateDef;
   489→    if (stateDef?.transitions) {
   490→      // Pick first available action
   491→      const action = Object.keys(stateDef.transitions)[0];
   492→      if (action) {
   493→        engine.interact(obj.id, action);
   494→        renderDebugStatus();
   495→
   496→        // Show brief feedback
   497→        showTooltip(e.clientX, e.clientY, `${obj.name}: ${action}`);
   498→      }
   499→    } else {
   500→      showTooltip(e.clientX, e.clientY, `${obj.name} [${obj.state}] — no actions`);
   501→    }
   502→  }
   503→});
   504→
   505→// Right-click: show all available actions for multi-action objects
   506→renderer.canvas.addEventListener('contextmenu', (e) => {
   507→  e.preventDefault();
   508→  const { tx, ty } = renderer.pageToTile(e.clientX, e.clientY);
   509→  const obj = findObjectAtTile(tx, ty);
   510→
   511→  if (obj) {
   512→    const stateDef = obj.currentStateDef;
   513→    const actions = stateDef?.transitions ? Object.keys(stateDef.transitions) : [];
   514→    const timerInfo = obj.timeRemainingMs !== null
   515→      ? ` (${(obj.timeRemainingMs / 1000).toFixed(0)}s remaining)`
   516→      : '';
   517→    showTooltip(e.clientX, e.clientY,
   518→      `${obj.name} [${obj.state}]${timerInfo}
Actions: ${actions.length ? actions.join(', ') : 'none'}`,
   519→      2000
   520→    );
   521→  }
   522→});
   523→
   524→function findObjectAtTile(tx, ty) {
   525→  const objects = engine.getAllObjects();
   526→  for (const obj of objects) {
   527→    const otx = obj.position.tx;
   528→    const oty = obj.position.ty;
   529→    const otw = obj.position.tw || 1;
   530→    const oth = obj.position.th || 1;
   531→    if (tx >= otx && tx < otx + otw && ty >= oty && ty < oty + oth) {
   532→      return obj;
   533→    }
   534→  }
   535→  return null;
   536→}
   537→
   538→function showTooltip(x, y, text, duration = 1200) {
   539→  tooltip.style.left = (x + 12) + 'px';
   540→  tooltip.style.top = (y - 20) + 'px';
   541→  tooltip.textContent = text;
   542→  tooltip.style.display = 'block';
   543→  clearTimeout(tooltip._hideTimer);
   544→  tooltip._hideTimer = setTimeout(() => {
   545→    tooltip.style.display = 'none';
   546→  }, duration);
   547→}
   548→
   549→// ── Utilities ──
   550→function formatMs(ms) {
   551→  const totalSec = Math.floor(ms / 1000);
   552→  const min = Math.floor(totalSec / 60);
   553→  const sec = totalSec % 60;
   554→  return min > 0 ? `${min}m ${sec}s` : `${sec}s`;
   555→}
   556→
   557→// ── Preload sprites, then start render loop ──
   558→preloadSprites().then(sprites => {
   559→  renderer.setSprites(sprites);
   560→  console.log(`Sprites loaded: ${sprites.tiles.size} tiles, ${sprites.objects.size} objects, ${sprites.characters.size} characters`);
   561→}).catch(err => {
   562→  console.warn('Sprite preload failed, using color fallback:', err);
   563→}).finally(() => {
   564→  renderer.startLoop(
   565→    () => engine.getAllObjects(),
   566→    () => engine.getCharacters(),
   567→    { showDebug: () => debugVisible, getSimClock: () => engine.simClock }
   568→  );
   569→});
   570→
   571→// ── Click-to-start overlay ──
   572→const overlay = $('start-overlay');
   573→const muteBtn = $('mute-btn');
   574→
   575→overlay.addEventListener('click', () => {
   576→  overlay.classList.add('hidden');
   577→  muteBtn.textContent = audio.muted ? '🔇' : '🔊';
   578→  muteBtn.classList.add('visible');
   579→  engine.start();
   580→  audio.start();
   581→  renderDebugStatus();
   582→  console.log('AI Aquarium started');
   583→});
   584→
   585→// ── Mute toggle ──
   586→muteBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
   587→  const muted = audio.toggleMute();
   588→  muteBtn.textContent = muted ? '🔇' : '🔊';
   589→  localStorage.setItem('ai-aquarium-muted', muted);
   590→});
   591→
   592→// ── Initial state ──
   593→renderDebugStatus();
   594→renderDebugLog();
   595→
   596→console.log('AI Aquarium — Click to start');
   597→console.log('Objects:', engine.getAllObjects().map(o => `${o.id} [${o.state}]`).join(', '));
   598→console.log('Characters:', engine.getCharacters().map(c => `${c.name} at (${c.tx},${c.ty})`).join(', '));
   599→</script>
   600→</body>
   601→</html>
   602→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

position: fixed;\n   151\u2192      inset: 0;\n   152\u2192      background: rgba(6, 6, 16, 0.92);\n   153\u2192      display: flex;\n   154\u2192      flex-direction: column;\n   155\u2192 161\u2192    #start-overlay .title {\n   162\u2192      font-family: 'Courier New', monospace;\n   163\u2192      font-size: 36px;\n   164\u2192      color: #e0ddd4;\n   165\u2192      letter-spacing: ease-in-out infinite;\n   175\u2192    }\n   176\u2192    @keyframes pulse {\n   177\u2192      0%, 100% { opacity: 0.5; }\n   178\u2192      50% { opacity: 1; }\n   179\u2192    }\n   180\u2192 208\u2192<body>\n   209\u2192\n   210\u2192<div id=\"start-overlay\">\n   211\u2192  <div class=\"title\">AI Aquarium</div>\n   212\u2192  <div class=\"subtitle\">Click anywhere to start</div>\n   213\u2192</div>\n   214\u2192\n   215\u2192<button id=\"mute-btn\" aria-label=\"Toggle audio\">\ud83d\udd0a</button>\n   216\u2192\n   217\u2192<div id=\"canvas-container\"></div>\n   218\u2192\n   219\u2192<div id=\"debug-overlay\">\n   220\u2192  <h2>AI Aquarium Debug <span id=\"debugToggleHint\" style=\"color:#666; font-weight:normal\">[D to toggle]</span></h2>\n   221\u2192  <div class=\"debug-controls\">\n   222\u2192    <button id=\"startBtn\">Start</button>\n   223\u2192    <button id=\"pauseBtn\">Pause</button>\n   224\u2192    <button id=\"stopBtn\">Stop</button>\n   225\u2192    <button id=\"tickBtn\">+1 Tick</button>\n   226\u2192    <button id=\"tick10Btn\">+10</button>\n   227\u2192    <span style=\"color:#666\">|</span>\n   228\u2192    <button id=\"speed1Btn\" class=\"active\">1x</button>\n   229\u2192    <button id=\"speed5Btn\">5x</button>\n   230\u2192    <button id=\"speed20Btn\">20x</button>\n   231\u2192    <span style=\"color:#666\">|</span>\n   232\u2192    <button id=\"aiToggleBtn\">AI: Off</button>\n   233\u2192    <span style=\"color:#666\">|</span>\n   234\u2192    <button id=\"resetBtn\">Reset</button>\n   235\u2192  </div>\n   236\u2192  <div class=\"debug-api\">\n   237\u2192    <label style=\"color:#888; font-size:10px\">API:</label>\n   238\u2192    <input id=\"apiEndpoint\" type=\"text\" placeholder=\"http://localhost:8787\">\n   239\u2192    <span id=\"apiWarning\" class=\"api-warn\">&#9888; No endpoint set</span>\n   240\u2192  </div>\n   241\u2192  <div class=\"debug-status\" id=\"debugStatus\">\n   242\u2192    <span>State: <span id=\"engineState\" class=\"stopped\">stopped</span></span>\n   243\u2192    <span>Tick: <span id=\"tickCount\">0</span></span>\n   244\u2192    <span>Elapsed: <span id=\"elapsedTime\">0s</span></span>\n   245\u2192    <span>Speed: <span id=\"timeScale\">1x</span></span>\n   246\u2192    <span>Clock: <span id=\"simClockDisplay\">Day 1 8:00 AM</span></span>\n   247\u2192    <span>AI: <span id=\"aiModeDisplay\" style=\"color:#c77\">off</span></span>\n   248\u2192  </div>\n   249\u2192  <div class=\"debug-chars\" id=\"charStatus\"></div>\n   250\u2192  <div class=\"debug-log\" id=\"debugLog\"></div>\n   251\u2192</div>\n   252\u2192\n   253\u2192<div id=\"click-tooltip\"></div>\n   254\u2192<div id=\"hint\">Press D for debug</div>\n   255\u2192\n   256\u2192<script type=\"module\">\n   257\u2192import { SimulationEngine } from './src/engine.js';\n   258\u2192import { Renderer, preloadSprites } from './src/renderer.js';\n   259\u2192import { TILE_SIZE } from './src/tilemap.js';\n   260\u2192import { WORLD_TREE } from './src/world.js';\n   261\u2192import { AIDecisionEngine } from './src/ai.js';\n   262\u2192import { AudioEngine } from './src/audio.js';\n   263\u2192\n   264\u2192// \u2500\u2500 AI setup \u2500\u2500\n   265\u2192const aiEngine = new AIDecisionEngine();\n   266\u2192\n   267\u2192// \u2500\u2500 Audio setup \u2500\u2500\n   268\u2192const audio = new AudioEngine();\n   269\u2192// Restore mute preference\n   270\u2192const savedMute = localStorage.getItem('ai-aquarium-muted');\n   271\u2192if (savedMute === 'true') {\n   272\u2192  audio.muted = true;\n   273\u2192}\n   274\u2192\n   275\u2192// \u2500\u2500 State \u2500\u2500\n   276\u2192let engine = new SimulationEngine({ aiEngine });\n   277\u2192let debugVisible = false;\n   278\u2192const eventLog = [];\n   279\u2192const MAX_LOG = 30;\n   280\u2192\n   281\u2192// \u2500\u2500 Renderer setup \u2500\u2500\n   282\u2192const container = document.getElementById('canvas-container');\n   283\u2192const renderer = new Renderer(container);\n   284\u2192\n   285\u2192// \u2500\u2500 DOM refs \u2500\u2500\n   286\u2192const $ = (id) => document.getElementById(id);\n   287\u2192const debugOverlay = $('debug-overlay');\n   288\u2192const tooltip = $('click-tooltip');\n   289\u2192\n   290\u2192// \u2500\u2500 Debug toggle \u2500\u2500\n   291\u2192document.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {\n   292\u2192  if (e.key === 'd' || e.key === 'D') {\n   293\u2192    debugVisible = !debugVisible;\n   294\u2192    debugOverlay.classList.toggle('visible', debugVisible);\n   295\u2192  }\n   296\u2192});\n   297\u2192\n   298\u2192// \u2500\u2500 Engine event listeners \u2500\u2500\n   299\u2192function attachEngineListeners() {\n   300\u2192  engine.on('object:transition', (event) => {\n   301\u2192    eventLog.unshift(event);\n   302\u2192    if (eventLog.length > MAX_LOG) eventLog.pop();\n   303\u2192    renderDebugLog();\n   304\u2192\n   305\u2192    // Trigger emoji float on the renderer\n   306\u2192    if (event.emoji) {\n   307\u2192      const obj = engine.getObject(event.objectId);\n   308\u2192      if (obj) {\n   309\u2192        renderer.addEmojiFloat(obj.position.tx, obj.position.ty, event.emoji);\n   310\u2192      }\n   311\u2192    }\n   312\u2192  });\n   313\u2192\n   314\u2192  engine.on('character:interact', (event) => {\n   315\u2192    eventLog.unshift({\n   316\u2192      objectName: event.objectName,\n   317\u2192      from: event.from,\n   318\u2192      to: event.to,\n   319\u2192      trigger: event.characterName,\n   320\u2192      emoji: event.emoji || null,\n   321\u2192    });\n   322\u2192    if (eventLog.length > MAX_LOG) eventLog.pop();\n   323\u2192    renderDebugLog();\n   324\u2192\n   325\u2192    // Emoji float on character for production events\n   326\u2192    if (event.produces) {\n   327\u2192      const char = engine.getCharacters().find(c => c.id === event.characterId);\n   328\u2192      if (char) {\n   329\u2192        const emoji = event.produces === 'bread' ? '\ud83c\udf5e' :\n   330\u2192                      event.produces === 'burnt_bread' ? '\ud83d\udca8' : '\u2728';\n   331\u2192        renderer.addEmojiFloat(Math.round(char.tx), Math.round(char.ty), emoji);\n   332\u2192      }\n   333\u2192    }\n   334\u2192  });\n   335\u2192\n   336\u2192  engine.on('engine:tick', () => {\n   337\u2192    // Update status bar with sim clock + last event\n   338\u2192    const clock = engine.simClock;\n   339\u2192    const status = engine.lastStatusText || '';\n   340\u2192    const line = status\n   341\u2192      ? `${clock.dayTimeString} \\u2014 ${status}`\n   342\u2192      : clock.dayTimeString;\n   343\u2192    renderer.setStatus(line);\n   344\u2192\n   345\u2192    // Sync audio ambient layers with time of day\n   346\u2192    audio.setTimeOfDay(clock.minuteOfDay);\n   347\u2192\n   348\u2192    renderDebugStatus();\n   349\u2192  });\n   350\u2192\n   351\u2192  engine.on('ai:speech', (event) => {\n   352\u2192    const char = engine.getCharacters().find(c => c.id === event.characterId);\n   353\u2192    if (char) {\n   354\u2192      renderer.addCharacterSpeech(char, event.dialogue, 5000);\n   355\u2192    }\n   356\u2192  });\n   357\u2192\n   358\u2192  engine.on('ai:status', (event) => {\n   359\u2192    engine.lastStatusText = `${event.characterName} ${event.description}`;\n   360\u2192  });\n   361\u2192}\n   362\u2192attachEngineListeners();\n   363\u2192\n   364\u2192// \u2500\u2500 Debug controls \u2500\u2500\n   365\u2192$('startBtn').onclick = () => { engine.start(); renderDebugStatus(); };\n   366\u2192$('pauseBtn').onclick = () => {\n   367\u2192  if (engine.paused) engine.resume(); else engine.pause();\n   368\u2192  renderDebugStatus();\n   369\u2192};\n   370\u2192$('stopBtn').onclick = () => { engine.stop(); renderDebugStatus(); };\n   371\u2192$('tickBtn').onclick = () => { engine.manualTick(1); renderDebugStatus(); };\n   372\u2192$('tick10Btn').onclick = () => { engine.manualTick(10); renderDebugStatus(); };\n   373\u2192\n   374\u2192function setSpeed(scale) {\n   375\u2192  engine.timeScale = scale;\n   376\u2192  $('speed1Btn').classList.toggle('active', scale === 1);\n   377\u2192  $('speed5Btn').classList.toggle('active', scale === 5);\n   378\u2192  $('speed20Btn').classList.toggle('active', scale === 20);\n   379\u2192  renderDebugStatus();\n   380\u2192}\n   381\u2192$('speed1Btn').onclick = () => setSpeed(1);\n   382\u2192$('speed5Btn').onclick = () => setSpeed(5);\n   383\u2192$('speed20Btn').onclick = () => setSpeed(20);\n   384\u2192\n   385\u2192// \u2500\u2500 AI controls \u2500\u2500\n   386\u2192const apiInput = $('apiEndpoint');\n   387\u2192const apiWarning = $('apiWarning');\n   388\u2192\n   389\u2192apiInput.addEventListener('change', () => {\n   390\u2192  const url = apiInput.value.trim();\n   391\u2192  aiEngine.setEndpoint(url);\n   392\u2192  apiWarning.style.display = url ? 'none' : 'inline';\n   393\u2192  if (engine.aiMode && !url) {\n   394\u2192    apiWarning.textContent = '\\u26A0 AI on but no endpoint \\u2014 will wander randomly';\n   395\u2192    apiWarning.style.display = 'inline';\n   396\u2192  }\n   397\u2192});\n   398\u2192\n   399\u2192$('aiToggleBtn').onclick = () => {\n   400\u2192  engine.aiMode = !engine.aiMode;\n   401\u2192  const btn = $('aiToggleBtn');\n   402\u2192  btn.textContent = `AI: ${engine.aiMode ? 'On' : 'Off'}`;\n   403\u2192  btn.classList.toggle('active', engine.aiMode);\n   404\u2192\n   405\u2192  const url = apiInput.value.trim();\n   406\u2192  if (engine.aiMode && !url) {\n   407\u2192    apiWarning.textContent = '\\u26A0 AI on but no endpoint \\u2014 will wander randomly';\n   408\u2192    apiWarning.style.display = 'inline';\n   409\u2192  } else if (url) {\n   410\u2192    apiWarning.style.display = 'none';\n   411\u2192  }\n   412\u2192  renderDebugStatus();\n   413\u2192};\n   414\u2192\n   415\u2192$('resetBtn').onclick = () => {\n   416\u2192  engine.stop();\n   417\u2192  engine = new SimulationEngine({ aiEngine });\n   418\u2192  // Preserve AI mode state across reset\n   419\u2192  const aiOn = $('aiToggleBtn').textContent.includes('On');\n   420\u2192  engine.aiMode = aiOn;\n   421\u2192  eventLog.length = 0;\n   422\u2192  attachEngineListeners();\n   423\u2192  renderDebugStatus();\n   424\u2192  renderDebugLog();\n   425\u2192  renderer.setStatus('');\n   426\u2192};\n   427\u2192\n   428\u2192// \u2500\u2500 Debug status render \u2500\u2500\n   429\u2192function renderDebugStatus() {\n   430\u2192  const stateEl = $('engineState');\n   431\u2192  if (engine.running && !engine.paused) {\n   432\u2192    stateEl.textContent = 'running';\n   433\u2192    stateEl.className = 'running';\n   434\u2192  } else if (engine.paused) {\n   435\u2192    stateEl.textContent = 'paused';\n   436\u2192    stateEl.className = 'paused';\n   437\u2192  } else {\n   438\u2192    stateEl.textContent = 'stopped';\n   439\u2192    stateEl.className = 'stopped';\n   440\u2192  }\n   441\u2192  $('tickCount').textContent = engine.tickCount;\n   442\u2192  $('elapsedTime').textContent = formatMs(engine.elapsedMs);\n   443\u2192  $('timeScale').textContent = engine.timeScale + 'x';\n   444\u2192\n   445\u2192  $('simClockDisplay').textContent = engine.simClock.dayTimeString;\n   446\u2192  const aiDisplay = $('aiModeDisplay');\n   447\u2192  aiDisplay.textContent = engine.aiMode ? 'on' : 'off';\n   448\u2192  aiDisplay.style.color = engine.aiMode ? '#7c7' : '#c77';\n   449\u2192\n   450\u2192  renderCharStatus();\n   451\u2192}\n   452\u2192\n   453\u2192function renderDebugLog() {\n   454\u2192  const el = $('debugLog');\n   455\u2192  if (eventLog.length === 0) {\n   456\u2192    el.innerHTML = '<div style=\"color:#555\">No events yet</div>';\n   457\u2192    return;\n   458\u2192  }\n   459\u2192  let html = '';\n   460\u2192  for (const e of eventLog) {\n   461\u2192    const emoji = e.emoji || '';\n   462\u2192    html += `<div class=\"entry\">${emoji} <span class=\"obj-name\">${e.objectName}</span>: <span class=\"from\">${e.from}</span> \u2192 <span class=\"to\">${e.to}</span> <span class=\"trigger\">[${e.trigger}]</span></div>`;\n   463\u2192  }\n   464\u2192  el.innerHTML = html;\n   465\u2192}\n   466\u2192\n   467\u2192function renderCharStatus() {\n   468\u2192  const chars = engine.getCharacters();\n   469\u2192  let html = '<div style=\"color:#cc8; font-size:10px; margin-bottom:2px\">Characters:</div>';\n   470\u2192  for (const c of chars) {\n   471\u2192    const target = c.targetObjectId ? ` <span class=\"char-target\">\u2192 ${c.targetObjectId}</span>` : '';\n   472\u2192    const moodIcon = c.moodEmoji || '';\n   473\u2192    const lastObj = c.lastObjectId ? ` <span style=\"color:#555\">last:${c.lastObjectId}</span>` : '';\n   474\u2192    html += `<div style=\"padding:1px 0\"><span class=\"char-label\">${c.name}</span> ${moodIcon}: ` +\n   475\u2192      `<span class=\"char-action\">${c.action}</span> ` +\n   476\u2192      `<span style=\"color:#686\">[${c.mood}]</span> ` +\n   477\u2192      `(${Math.round(c.tx)},${Math.round(c.ty)})${target}${lastObj}</div>`;\n   478\u2192  }\n   479\u2192  $('charStatus').innerHTML = html;\n   480\u2192}\n   481\u2192\n   482\u2192// \u2500\u2500 Canvas click \u2192 find object at tile \u2192 trigger interaction \u2500\u2500\n   483\u2192renderer.canvas.addEventListener('click', (e) => {\n   484\u2192  const { tx, ty } = renderer.pageToTile(e.clientX, e.clientY);\n   485\u2192  const obj = findObjectAtTile(tx, ty);\n   486\u2192\n   487\u2192  if (obj) {\n   488\u2192    const stateDef = obj.currentStateDef;\n   489\u2192    if (stateDef?.transitions) {\n   490\u2192      // Pick first available action\n   491\u2192      const action = Object.keys(stateDef.transitions)[0];\n   492\u2192      if (action) {\n   493\u2192        engine.interact(obj.id, action);\n   494\u2192        renderDebugStatus();\n   495\u2192\n   496\u2192        // Show brief feedback\n   497\u2192        showTooltip(e.clientX, e.clientY, `${obj.name}: ${action}`);\n   498\u2192      }\n   499\u2192    } else {\n   500\u2192      showTooltip(e.clientX, e.clientY, `${obj.name} [${obj.state}] \u2014 no actions`);\n   501\u2192    }\n   502\u2192  }\n   503\u2192});\n   504\u2192\n   505\u2192// Right-click: show all available actions for multi-action objects\n   506\u2192renderer.canvas.addEventListener('contextmenu', (e) => {\n   507\u2192  e.preventDefault();\n   508\u2192  const { tx, ty } = renderer.pageToTile(e.clientX, e.clientY);\n   509\u2192  const obj = findObjectAtTile(tx, ty);\n   510\u2192\n   511\u2192  if (obj) {\n   512\u2192    const stateDef = obj.currentStateDef;\n   513\u2192    const actions = stateDef?.transitions ? Object.keys(stateDef.transitions) : [];\n   514\u2192    const timerInfo = obj.timeRemainingMs !== null\n   515\u2192      ? ` (${(obj.timeRemainingMs / 1000).toFixed(0)}s remaining)`\n   516\u2192      : '';\n   517\u2192    showTooltip(e.clientX, e.clientY,\n   518\u2192      `${obj.name} [${obj.state}]${timerInfo}\\nActions: ${actions.length ? actions.join(', ') : 'none'}`,\n   519\u2192      2000\n   520\u2192    );\n   521\u2192  }\n   522\u2192});\n   523\u2192\n   524\u2192function findObjectAtTile(tx, ty) {\n   525\u2192  const objects = engine.getAllObjects();\n   526\u2192  for (const obj of objects) {\n   527\u2192    const otx = obj.position.tx;\n   528\u2192    const oty = obj.position.ty;\n   529\u2192    const otw = obj.position.tw || 1;\n   530\u2192    const oth = obj.position.th || 1;\n   531\u2192    if (tx >= otx && tx < otx + otw && ty >= oty && ty < oty + oth) {\n   532\u2192      return obj;\n   533\u2192    }\n   534\u2192  }\n   535\u2192  return null;\n   536\u2192}\n   537\u2192\n   538\u2192function showTooltip(x, y, text, duration = 1200) {\n   539\u2192  tooltip.style.left = (x + 12) + 'px';\n   540\u2192  tooltip.style.top = (y - 20) + 'px';\n   541\u2192  tooltip.textContent = text;\n   542\u2192  tooltip.style.display = 'block';\n   543\u2192  clearTimeout(tooltip._hideTimer);\n   544\u2192  tooltip._hideTimer = setTimeout(() => {\n   545\u2192    tooltip.style.display = 'none';\n   546\u2192  }, duration);\n   547\u2192}\n   548\u2192\n   549\u2192// \u2500\u2500 Utilities \u2500\u2500\n   550\u2192function formatMs(ms) {\n   551\u2192  const totalSec = Math.floor(ms / 1000);\n   552\u2192  const min = Math.floor(totalSec / 60);\n   553\u2192  const sec = totalSec % 60;\n   554\u2192  return min > 0 ? `${min}m ${sec}s` : `${sec}s`;\n   555\u2192}\n   556\u2192\n   557\u2192// \u2500\u2500 Preload sprites, then start render loop \u2500\u2500\n   558\u2192preloadSprites().then(sprites => {\n   559\u2192  renderer.setSprites(sprites);\n   560\u2192  console.log(`Sprites loaded: ${sprites.tiles.size} tiles, ${sprites.objects.size} objects, ${sprites.characters.size} characters`);\n   561\u2192}).catch(err => {\n   562\u2192  console.warn('Sprite preload failed, using color fallback:', err);\n   563\u2192}).finally(() => {\n   564\u2192  renderer.startLoop(\n   565\u2192    () => engine.getAllObjects(),\n   566\u2192    () => engine.getCharacters(),\n   567\u2192    { showDebug: () => debugVisible, getSimClock: () => engine.simClock }\n   568\u2192  );\n   569\u2192});\n   570\u2192\n   571\u2192// \u2500\u2500 Click-to-start overlay \u2500\u2500\n   572\u2192const overlay = $('start-overlay');\n   573\u2192const muteBtn = $('mute-btn');\n   574\u2192\n   575\u2192overlay.addEventListener('click', () => {\n   576\u2192  overlay.classList.add('hidden');\n   577\u2192  muteBtn.textContent = audio.muted ? '\ud83d\udd07' : '\ud83d\udd0a';\n   578\u2192  muteBtn.classList.add('visible');\n   579\u2192  engine.start();\n   580\u2192  audio.start();\n   581\u2192  renderDebugStatus();\n   582\u2192  console.log('AI Aquarium started');\n   583\u2192});\n   584\u2192\n   585\u2192// \u2500\u2500 Mute toggle \u2500\u2500\n   586\u2192muteBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {\n   587\u2192  const muted = audio.toggleMute();\n   588\u2192  muteBtn.textContent = muted ? '\ud83d\udd07' : '\ud83d\udd0a';\n   589\u2192  localStorage.setItem('ai-aquarium-muted', muted);\n   590\u2192});\n   591\u2192\n   592\u2192// \u2500\u2500 Initial state \u2500\u2500\n   593\u2192renderDebugStatus();\n   594\u2192renderDebugLog();\n   595\u2192\n   596\u2192console.log('AI Aquarium \u2014 Click to start');\n   597\u2192console.log('Objects:', engine.getAllObjects().map(o => `${o.id} [${o.state}]`).join(', '));\n   598\u2192console.log('Characters:', engine.getCharacters().map(c => `${c.name} at (${c.tx},${c.ty})`).join(', '));\n   599\u2192</script>\n   600\u2192</body>\n   601\u2192</html>\n   602\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
  }
}

USERMESSAGE #79
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
Excellent! I have gathered enough information. Let me create a comprehensive summary based on my exploration.

## Summary: Character Facing/Direction Implementation

Based on my exploration of the AI Aquarium codebase, here's what I found:

### 1. **Facing Property Name and Valid Values**

**Property Name:** `facing` (on Character class)

**Valid Values:** `'up'`, `'down'`, `'left'`, `'right'`

- Initialized to `'down'` in the Character constructor (characters.js:117)
- Updated via the `_updateFacing(dx, dy)` method which calculates direction from deltas

### 2. **How Facing Direction Works**

**The `_updateFacing()` method** (characters.js:244-249):
```javascript
_updateFacing(dx, dy) {
  if (dx > 0) this.facing = 'right';
  else if (dx < 0) this.facing = 'left';
  else if (dy > 0) this.facing = 'down';
  else if (dy < 0) this.facing = 'up';
}
```

The logic prioritizes horizontal direction (left/right) over vertical, and uses delta X and Y values to determine which direction to face.

### 3. **Facing Toward Objects (FRG-244 Feature)**

**Implementation:** `_doInteraction()` in engine.js (lines 310-341)

When a character interacts with an object:
1. Calculate object center: `objCx = obj.position.tx + (obj.position.tw || 1) / 2`
2. Calculate deltas from character to object center
3. Prioritize horizontal/vertical based on which delta is larger in absolute value
4. Call `_updateFacing()` with appropriate deltas

Example:
```javascript
const objCx = obj.position.tx + (obj.position.tw || 1) / 2;
const objCy = obj.position.ty + (obj.position.th || 1) / 2;
const dx = objCx - char.tx;
const dy = objCy - char.ty;
if (Math.abs(dx) >= Math.abs(dy)) {
  char._updateFacing(dx, 0);  // Face horizontally
} else {
  char._updateFacing(0, dy);  // Face vertically
}
```

### 4. **Facing Toward Characters During Talk**

**Implementation:** In two places:

**A. Engine.js** (lines 297-305) - When a character arrives near another for conversation:
```javascript
const target = this.characters.find(c => c.id === char.targetCharId);
if (target) {
  char._updateFacing(target.tx - char.tx, target.ty - char.ty);
  target._updateFacing(char.tx - target.tx, char.ty - target.ty);
}
```

**B. AI.js** (lines 204-207) - When AI decides characters should talk:
```javascript
// Already nearby — face each other immediately
char._updateFacing(target.tx - char.tx, target.ty - char.ty);
target._updateFacing(char.tx - target.tx, char.ty - target.ty);
```

Both characters face each other using delta calculations.

### 5. **Character State Exposed on window**

**NOT directly exposed on `window.__SIM_ENGINE__` or `window._gameEngine`**

However, the engine variable is scoped in the index.html module scope and exposed via:
- `engine.getCharacters()` - returns character array
- `engine.getAllObjects()` - returns object array
- `engine.snapshot()` - returns full state snapshot

**Character properties exposed in snapshot:**
```javascript
{
  id: c.id, 
  name: c.name, 
  tx: c.tx,          // tile X
  ty: c.ty,          // tile Y
  action: c.action,  // 'idle' | 'walking' | 'interacting'
  facing: c.facing,  // 'up' | 'down' | 'left' | 'right'
  mood: c.mood,      // 'happy' | 'content' | 'neutral' | 'upset'
  targetObjectId: c.targetObjectId
}
```

### 6. **Object Position Properties**

Objects have a `position` property with:
- `tx` - tile X coordinate
- `ty` - tile Y coordinate
- `tw` (optional, default 1) - tile width
- `th` (optional, default 1) - tile height

Example from world.js:
- `cafe_display`: `position: { tx: 5, ty: 12, tw: 2, th: 1 }`
- `cafe_espresso`: `position: { tx: 2, ty: 11 }`

### Key Files:
- `/home/gem/ai-aquarium/src/characters.js` - Character class with facing property
- `/home/gem/ai-aquarium/src/engine.js` - Interaction and character-to-character facing logic
- `/home/gem/ai-aquarium/src/ai.js` - AI talk decision facing logic
- `/home/gem/ai-aquarium/index.html` - Engine initialization and window scope

        "type": "text",
        "text": "Excellent! I have gathered enough information. Let me create a comprehensive summary based on my exploration.\n\n## Summary: Character Facing/Direction Implementation\n\nBased on my exploration of the AI Aquarium codebase, here's what I found:\n\n### 1. **Facing Property Name and Valid Values**\n\n**Property Name:** `facing` (on Character class)\n\n**Valid Values:** `'up'`, `'down'`, `'left'`, `'right'`\n\n- Initialized to `'down'` in the Character constructor (characters.js:117)\n- Updated via the `_updateFacing(dx, dy)` method which calculates direction from deltas\n\n### 2. **How Facing Direction Works**\n\n**The `_updateFacing()` method** (characters.js:244-249):\n```javascript\n_updateFacing(dx, dy) {\n  if (dx > 0) this.facing = 'right';\n  else if (dx < 0) this.facing = 'left';\n  else if (dy > 0) this.facing = 'down';\n  else if (dy < 0) this.facing = 'up';\n}\n```\n\nThe logic prioritizes horizontal direction (left/right) over vertical, and uses delta X and Y values to determine which direction to face.\n\n### 3. **Facing Toward Objects (FRG-244 Feature)**\n\n**Implementation:** `_doInteraction()` in engine.js (lines 310-341)\n\nWhen a character interacts with an object:\n1. Calculate object center: `objCx = obj.position.tx + (obj.position.tw || 1) / 2`\n2. Calculate deltas from character to object center\n3. Prioritize horizontal/vertical based on which delta is larger in absolute value\n4. Call `_updateFacing()` with appropriate deltas\n\nExample:\n```javascript\nconst objCx = obj.position.tx + (obj.position.tw || 1) / 2;\nconst objCy = obj.position.ty + (obj.position.th || 1) / 2;\nconst dx = objCx - char.tx;\nconst dy = objCy - char.ty;\nif (Math.abs(dx) >= Math.abs(dy)) {\n  char._updateFacing(dx, 0);  // Face horizontally\n} else {\n  char._updateFacing(0, dy);  // Face vertically\n}\n```\n\n### 4. **Facing Toward Characters During Talk**\n\n**Implementation:** In two places:\n\n**A. Engine.js** (lines 297-305) - When a character arrives near another for conversation:\n```javascript\nconst target = this.characters.find(c => c.id === char.targetCharId);\nif (target) {\n  char._updateFacing(target.tx - char.tx, target.ty - char.ty);\n  target._updateFacing(char.tx - target.tx, char.ty - target.ty);\n}\n```\n\n**B. AI.js** (lines 204-207) - When AI decides characters should talk:\n```javascript\n// Already nearby \u2014 face each other immediately\nchar._updateFacing(target.tx - char.tx, target.ty - char.ty);\ntarget._updateFacing(char.tx - target.tx, char.ty - target.ty);\n```\n\nBoth characters face each other using delta calculations.\n\n### 5. **Character State Exposed on window**\n\n**NOT directly exposed on `window.__SIM_ENGINE__` or `window._gameEngine`**\n\nHowever, the engine variable is scoped in the index.html module scope and exposed via:\n- `engine.getCharacters()` - returns character array\n- `engine.getAllObjects()` - returns object array\n- `engine.snapshot()` - returns full state snapshot\n\n**Character properties exposed in snapshot:**\n```javascript\n{\n  id: c.id, \n  name: c.name, \n  tx: c.tx,          // tile X\n  ty: c.ty,          // tile Y\n  action: c.action,  // 'idle' | 'walking' | 'interacting'\n  facing: c.facing,  // 'up' | 'down' | 'left' | 'right'\n  mood: c.mood,      // 'happy' | 'content' | 'neutral' | 'upset'\n  targetObjectId: c.targetObjectId\n}\n```\n\n### 6. **Object Position Properties**\n\nObjects have a `position` property with:\n- `tx` - tile X coordinate\n- `ty` - tile Y coordinate\n- `tw` (optional, default 1) - tile width\n- `th` (optional, default 1) - tile height\n\nExample from world.js:\n- `cafe_display`: `position: { tx: 5, ty: 12, tw: 2, th: 1 }`\n- `cafe_espresso`: `position: { tx: 2, ty: 11 }`\n\n### Key Files:\n- `/home/gem/ai-aquarium/src/characters.js` - Character class with facing property\n- `/home/gem/ai-aquarium/src/engine.js` - Interaction and character-to-character facing logic\n- `/home/gem/ai-aquarium/src/ai.js` - AI talk decision facing logic\n- `/home/gem/ai-aquarium/index.html` - Engine initialization and window scope"
      },
      {
        "type": "text",
        "text": "agentId: a82d072200f0ff65b (for resuming to continue this agent's work if needed)\n<usage>total_tokens: 48479\ntool_uses: 12\nduration_ms: 28494</usage>"
      }
    ]
  }
}

461 /home/gem/ai-aquarium/src/engine.js
  const min = Math.floor(totalSec / 60);
  const sec = totalSec % 60;
  return min > 0 ? `${min}m ${sec}s` : `${sec}s`;
}

// ── Preload sprites, then start render loop ──
preloadSprites().then(sprites => {
  renderer.setSprites(sprites);
  console.log(`Sprites loaded: ${sprites.tiles.size} tiles, ${sprites.objects.size} objects, ${sprites.characters.size} characters`);
}).catch(err => {
  console.warn('Sprite preload failed, using color fallback:', err);
}).finally(() => {
  renderer.startLoop(
    () => engine.getAllObjects(),
    () => engine.getCharacters(),
    { showDebug: () => debugVisible, getSimClock: () => engine.simClock }
  );
});

// ── Click-to-start overlay ──
const overlay = $('start-overlay');
const muteBtn = $('mute-btn');

overlay.addEventListener('click', () => {
  overlay.classList.add('hidden');
  muteBtn.textContent = audio.muted ? '🔇' : '🔊';
  muteBtn.classList.add('visible');
  engine.start();
  audio.start();
  renderDebugStatus();
  console.log('AI Aquarium started');
});

// ── Mute toggle ──
muteBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
  const muted = audio.toggleMute();
  muteBtn.textContent = muted ? '🔇' : '🔊';
  localStorage.setItem('ai-aquarium-muted', muted);
});

// ── Initial state ──
renderDebugStatus();
renderDebugLog();

console.log('AI Aquarium — Click to start');
console.log('Objects:', engine.getAllObjects().map(o => `${o.id} [${o.state}]`).join(', '));
console.log('Characters:', engine.getCharacters().map(c => `${c.name} at (${c.tx},${c.ty})`).join(', '));
</script>
</body>
</html>

USERMESSAGE #115
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→'use strict';
     2→/**
     3→ * SMOKE — FRG-184
     4→ * Verifies the sim page loads, Phaser boots, and all 3 characters are present.
     5→ */
     6→const { test, expect } = require('@playwright/test');
     7→const { waitForEngine, getEngineState } = require('../helpers');
     8→
     9→test.describe('Smoke: App loads and sim initialises', () => {
    10→  test.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {
    11→    await page.goto('/');
    12→    await waitForEngine(page);
    13→  });
    14→
    15→  test('page title is correct', async ({ page }) => {
    16→    await expect(page).toHaveTitle(/AI Aquarium/i);
    17→  });
    18→
    19→  test('Phaser canvas renders', async ({ page }) => {
    20→    const canvas = page.locator('canvas');
    21→    await expect(canvas).toBeVisible();
    22→  });
    23→
    24→  test('sim clock is visible and ticking', async ({ page }) => {
    25→    // Clock text is rendered by Phaser into the canvas — read via engine
    26→    const state1 = await getEngineState(page);
    27→    expect(state1.simTime).toMatch(/^\d+:\d{2} (AM|PM)$/);
    28→
    29→    // Verify via raw simTimeMs (more precise than string comparison)
    30→    // simSpeed = 60 → 1 real second = 60 sim seconds
    31→    const ms0 = await page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.simTimeMs);
    32→    // Wait 5 s → expect ~300 sim seconds (5 real min) of advancement
    33→    await page.waitForTimeout(5000);
    34→    const ms1 = await page.evaluate(() => window._gameEngine.simTimeMs);
    35→
    36→    expect(ms1, 'simTimeMs did not advance — Phaser update loop may not be running').toBeGreaterThan(ms0);
    37→  });
    38→
    39→  test('exactly 3 characters are loaded', async ({ page }) => {
    40→    const state = await getEngineState(page);
    41→    expect(state.characters).toHaveLength(3);
    42→  });
    43→
    44→  test('characters have expected names', async ({ page }) => {
    45→    const state = await getEngineState(page);
    46→    const labels = state.characters.map(c => c.label).sort();
    47→    expect(labels).toEqual(['Maple', 'Nyx', 'Sol']);
    48→  });
    49→
    50→  test('AI engine is enabled', async ({ page }) => {
    51→    const state = await getEngineState(page);
    52→    expect(state.aiEnabled).toBe(true);
    53→  });
    54→
    55→  test('all 10 interactive objects are loaded', async ({ page }) => {
    56→    const state = await getEngineState(page);
    57→    expect(state.objectStates).toHaveLength(10);
    58→    const ids = state.objectStates.map(o => o.id).sort();
    59→    expect(ids).toEqual([
    60→      'bed', 'bonsai', 'bookshelf', 'desk', 'easel',
    61→      'fridge', 'kotatsu', 'stove', 'tea_set', 'tv',
    62→    ]);
    63→  });
    64→
    65→  test('debug panel opens and shows object states', async ({ page }) => {
    66→    const debugBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /debug/i });
    67→    await expect(debugBtn).toBeVisible();
    68→    await debugBtn.click();
    69→    await expect(page.getByText('OBJECT STATES')).toBeVisible();
    70→    await expect(page.getByText('Stove')).toBeVisible();
    71→  });
    72→});
    73→

Let me understand the approach better: 1. The task says to use `window.__SIM_ENGINE__` to access characters 2. But neither `window.__SIM_ENGINE__` nor `window._gameEngine` are exposed in the local source code I need to look at how to access the engine. Maybe I need to inject a script to expose the engine, or use a different approach. Let me look at the index.html more carefully to find how the engine is accessible. The task mentions `window.__SIM_ENGINE__` as the interface, but the existing tests reference `window._gameEngine`, which suggests the naming convention might have changed between versions. The real issue is that the engine variable is scoped locally in index.html, so I need to figure out how to expose it for testing purposes. I'm noticing a discrepancy between what the task instructions say about `window.__SIM_ENGINE__` and what I'm actually finding in the code. The working Vercel tests reference `window._gameEngine`, which suggests the deployment exposes the engine differently than the local version. Let me check the full index.html to see what global variable is actually being set.

USERMESSAGE #129
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
   255→
   256→<script type="module">
   257→import { SimulationEngine } from './src/engine.js';
   258→import { Renderer, preloadSprites } from './src/renderer.js';
   259→import { TILE_SIZE } from './src/tilemap.js';
   260→import { WORLD_TREE } from './src/world.js';
   261→import { AIDecisionEngine } from './src/ai.js';
   262→import { AudioEngine } from './src/audio.js';
   263→
   264→// ── AI setup ──
   265→const aiEngine = new AIDecisionEngine();
   266→
   267→// ── Audio setup ──
   268→const audio = new AudioEngine();
   269→// Restore mute preference
   270→const savedMute = localStorage.getItem('ai-aquarium-muted');
   271→if (savedMute === 'true') {
   272→  audio.muted = true;
   273→}
   274→
   275→// ── State ──
   276→let engine = new SimulationEngine({ aiEngine });
   277→let debugVisible = false;
   278→const eventLog = [];
   279→const MAX_LOG = 30;
   280→
   281→// ── Renderer setup ──
   282→const container = document.getElementById('canvas-container');
   283→const renderer = new Renderer(container);
   284→
   285→// ── DOM refs ──
   286→const $ = (id) => document.getElementById(id);
   287→const debugOverlay = $('debug-overlay');
   288→const tooltip = $('click-tooltip');
   289→
   290→// ── Debug toggle ──
   291→document.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {
   292→  if (e.key === 'd' || e.key === 'D') {
   293→    debugVisible = !debugVisible;
   294→    debugOverlay.classList.toggle('visible', debugVisible);
   295→  }
   296→});
   297→
   298→// ── Engine event listeners ──
   299→function attachEngineListeners() {
   300→  engine.on('object:transition', (event) => {
   301→    eventLog.unshift(event);
   302→    if (eventLog.length > MAX_LOG) eventLog.pop();
   303→    renderDebugLog();
   304→
   305→    // Trigger emoji float on the renderer
   306→    if (event.emoji) {
   307→      const obj = engine.getObject(event.objectId);
   308→      if (obj) {
   309→        renderer.addEmojiFloat(obj.position.tx, obj.position.ty, event.emoji);
   310→      }
   311→    }
   312→  });
   313→
   314→  engine.on('character:interact', (event) => {
   315→    eventLog.unshift({
   316→      objectName: event.objectName,
   317→      from: event.from,
   318→      to: event.to,
   319→      trigger: event.characterName,
   320→      emoji: event.emoji || null,
   321→    });
   322→    if (eventLog.length > MAX_LOG) eventLog.pop();
   323→    renderDebugLog();
   324→
   325→    // Emoji float on character for production events
   326→    if (event.produces) {
   327→      const char = engine.getCharacters().find(c => c.id === event.characterId);
   328→      if (char) {
   329→        const emoji = event.produces === 'bread' ? '🍞' :
   330→                      event.produces === 'burnt_bread' ? '💨' : '✨';
   331→        renderer.addEmojiFloat(Math.round(char.tx), Math.round(char.ty), emoji);
   332→      }
   333→    }
   334→  });

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

'./src/renderer.js';\n   259\u2192import { TILE_SIZE } from './src/tilemap.js';\n   260\u2192import { WORLD_TREE } from './src/world.js';\n   261\u2192import { AIDecisionEngine } from './src/ai.js';\n   262\u2192import { AudioEngine } from './src/audio.js';\n   263\u2192\n   264\u2192// \u2500\u2500 AI setup \u2500\u2500\n   265\u2192const aiEngine = new AIDecisionEngine();\n   266\u2192\n   267\u2192// \u2500\u2500 Audio setup \u2500\u2500\n   268\u2192const audio = new AudioEngine();\n   269\u2192// Restore mute preference\n   270\u2192const savedMute = localStorage.getItem('ai-aquarium-muted');\n   271\u2192if (savedMute === 'true') {\n   272\u2192  audio.muted = true;\n   273\u2192}\n   274\u2192\n   275\u2192// \u2500\u2500 State \u2500\u2500\n   276\u2192let engine = new SimulationEngine({ aiEngine });\n   277\u2192let debugVisible = false;\n   278\u2192const eventLog = [];\n   279\u2192const MAX_LOG = 30;\n   280\u2192\n   281\u2192// \u2500\u2500 Renderer setup \u2500\u2500\n   282\u2192const container = document.getElementById('canvas-container');\n   283\u2192const renderer = new Renderer(container);\n   284\u2192\n   285\u2192// \u2500\u2500 DOM refs \u2500\u2500\n   286\u2192const $ = (id) => document.getElementById(id);\n   287\u2192const debugOverlay = $('debug-overlay');\n   288\u2192const tooltip = $('click-tooltip');\n   289\u2192\n   290\u2192// \u2500\u2500 Debug toggle \u2500\u2500\n   291\u2192document.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {\n   292\u2192  if (e.key === 'd' || e.key === 'D') {\n   293\u2192    debugVisible = !debugVisible;\n   294\u2192    debugOverlay.classList.toggle('visible', debugVisible);\n   295\u2192  }\n   296\u2192});\n   297\u2192\n   298\u2192// \u2500\u2500 Engine event listeners \u2500\u2500\n   299\u2192function attachEngineListeners() {\n   300\u2192  engine.on('object:transition', (event) => {\n   301\u2192    eventLog.unshift(event);\n   302\u2192    if (eventLog.length > MAX_LOG) eventLog.pop();\n   303\u2192    renderDebugLog();\n   304\u2192\n   305\u2192    // Trigger emoji float on the renderer\n   306\u2192    if (event.emoji) {\n   307\u2192      const obj = engine.getObject(event.objectId);\n   308\u2192      if (obj) {\n   309\u2192        renderer.addEmojiFloat(obj.position.tx, obj.position.ty, event.emoji);\n   310\u2192      }\n   311\u2192    }\n   312\u2192  });\n   313\u2192\n   314\u2192  engine.on('character:interact', (event) => {\n   315\u2192    eventLog.unshift({\n   316\u2192      objectName: event.objectName,\n   317\u2192      from: event.from,\n   318\u2192      to: event.to,\n   319\u2192      trigger: event.characterName,\n   320\u2192      emoji: event.emoji || null,\n   321\u2192    });\n   322\u2192    if (eventLog.length > MAX_LOG) eventLog.pop();\n   323\u2192    renderDebugLog();\n   324\u2192\n   325\u2192    // Emoji float on character for production events\n   326\u2192    if (event.produces) {\n   327\u2192      const char = engine.getCharacters().find(c => c.id === event.characterId);\n   328\u2192      if (char) {\n   329\u2192        const emoji = event.produces === 'bread' ? '\ud83c\udf5e' :\n   330\u2192                      event.produces === 'burnt_bread' ? '\ud83d\udca8' : '\u2728';\n   331\u2192        renderer.addEmojiFloat(Math.round(char.tx), Math.round(char.ty), emoji);\n   332\u2192      }\n   333\u2192    }\n   334\u2192  });\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware.   }

   357→
   358→  engine.on('ai:status', (event) => {
   359→    engine.lastStatusText = `${event.characterName} ${event.description}`;
   360→  });
   361→}
   362→attachEngineListeners();
   363→
   364→// ── Debug controls ──
   365→$('startBtn').onclick = () => { engine.start(); renderDebugStatus(); };
   366→$('pauseBtn').onclick = () => {
   367→  if (engine.paused) engine.resume(); else engine.pause();
   368→  renderDebugStatus();
   369→};
   370→$('stopBtn').onclick = () => { engine.stop(); renderDebugStatus(); };
   371→$('tickBtn').onclick = () => { engine.manualTick(1); renderDebugStatus(); };
   372→$('tick10Btn').onclick = () => { engine.manualTick(10); renderDebugStatus(); };
   373→
   374→function setSpeed(scale) {
   375→  engine.timeScale = scale;
   376→  $('speed1Btn').classList.toggle('active', scale === 1);
   377→  $('speed5Btn').classList.toggle('active', scale === 5);
   378→  $('speed20Btn').classList.toggle('active', scale === 20);
   379→  renderDebugStatus();
   380→}
   381→$('speed1Btn').onclick = () => setSpeed(1);
   382→$('speed5Btn').onclick = () => setSpeed(5);
   383→$('speed20Btn').onclick = () => setSpeed(20);
   384→
   385→// ── AI controls ──
   386→const apiInput = $('apiEndpoint');
   387→const apiWarning = $('apiWarning');
   388→
   389→apiInput.addEventListener('change', () => {
   390→  const url = apiInput.value.trim();
   391→  aiEngine.setEndpoint(url);
   392→  apiWarning.style.display = url ? 'none' : 'inline';
   393→  if (engine.aiMode && !url) {
   394→    apiWarning.textContent = '\u26A0 AI on but no endpoint \u2014 will wander randomly';
   395→    apiWarning.style.display = 'inline';
   396→  }
   397→});
   398→
   399→$('aiToggleBtn').onclick = () => {
   400→  engine.aiMode = !engine.aiMode;
   401→  const btn = $('aiToggleBtn');
   402→  btn.textContent = `AI: ${engine.aiMode ? 'On' : 'Off'}`;
   403→  btn.classList.toggle('active', engine.aiMode);
   404→
   405→  const url = apiInput.value.trim();
   406→  if (engine.aiMode && !url) {
   407→    apiWarning.textContent = '\u26A0 AI on but no endpoint \u2014 will wander randomly';
   408→    apiWarning.style.display = 'inline';
   409→  } else if (url) {
   410→    apiWarning.style.display = 'none';
   411→  }
   412→  renderDebugStatus();
   413→};
   414→
   415→$('resetBtn').onclick = () => {
   416→  engine.stop();
   417→  engine = new SimulationEngine({ aiEngine });
   418→  // Preserve AI mode state across reset
   419→  const aiOn = $('aiToggleBtn').textContent.includes('On');
   420→  engine.aiMode = aiOn;
   421→  eventLog.length = 0;
   422→  attachEngineListeners();
   423→  renderDebugStatus();
   424→  renderDebugLog();
   425→  renderer.setStatus('');
   426→};
   427→
   428→// ── Debug status render ──
   429→function renderDebugStatus() {
   430→  const stateEl = $('engineState');
   431→  if (engine.running && !engine.paused) {
   432→    stateEl.textContent = 'running';
   433→    stateEl.className = 'running';
   434→  } else if (engine.paused) {

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #132
The engine is not exposed on `window._gameEngine` in this version of the app. The `let engine` variable is module-scoped. I need to figure out how to access the engine state for my tests. Options: 1. Look for another way to access engine in the app - maybe there's a debug.html that exposes it differently 2. Check if `getCharacters()` or similar is attached to some global 3. Use a different approach - maybe wait for DOM events or read from the debug dashboard HTML Looking at the existing tests: they all use `window._gameEngine`. This was presumably added in an older version of the Vercel app. The task mentions the game exposes `window.__SIM_ENGINE__`, so let me check if that's available in this branch. Actually, I'm realizing the existing tests were built for the Vercel deployment, but I need to write new tests that work against the local server, which may not expose the engine the same way. I should focus on what the local version actually provides rather than relying on the old test setup.

     2→<html lang="en">

     5→  <title>Debug — Static Scene</title>
     6→  <style>
     7→    * { margin: 0; padding: 0; box-sizing: border-box; }
     8→    body {
     9→      background: #1a1a2e;
    10→      padding: 16px;
    11→      font-family: 'Courier New', monospace;
    12→      color: #e0ddd4;
    13→    }
    14→    h1 { font-size: 13px; color: #FFD700; margin-bottom: 6px; }
    15→    #status { font-size: 11px; color: #aaa; margin-bottom: 10px; }
    16→    #legend {
    17→      font-size: 10px; color: #ccc;
    18→      margin-bottom: 12px;
    19→      display: flex; gap: 16px; flex-wrap: wrap;
    20→    }
    21→    .leg { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 4px; }
    22→    .dot {
    23→      width: 10px; height: 10px; border-radius: 50%; display: inline-block;
    24→    }
    25→    #wrap { position: relative; display: inline-block; }
    26→    canvas { display: block; image-rendering: pixelated; }
    27→    #label-canvas {
    28→      position: absolute; top: 0; left: 0;
    29→      pointer-events: none;
    30→    }
    31→  </style>

display: inline-block;\n    24\u2192    }\n    25\u2192    #wrap { position: relative; display: inline-block; }\n    26\u2192    canvas { display: block; image-rendering: pixelated; }\n    27\u2192    #label-canvas {\n    28\u2192      position: absolute; top: 0; left: 0;\n    29\u2192      pointer-events: none;\n    30\u2192    }\n    31\u2192  </style>\n    32\u2192</head>\n    33\u2192<body>\n    34\u2192  <h1>Debug \u2014 Static Scene Render (1\u00d7 scale, no simulation)</h1>\n    35\u2192  <div id=\"status\">Loading sprites\u2026</div>\n    36\u2192  <div id=\"legend\">\n    37\u2192    <span class=\"leg\"><span class=\"dot\" style=\"background:#ffe080\"></span>Objects (interactive)</span>\n    38\u2192    <span class=\"leg\"><span class=\"dot\" style=\"background:#80c8ff\"></span>Props (decorative)</span>\n    39\u2192    <span class=\"leg\"><span class=\"dot\" style=\"background:#ff8080\"></span>Characters (start pos)</span>\n    40\u2192    <span class=\"leg\">Grid lines show tile boundaries \u00b7 col,row coords top-left of each tile</span>\n    41\u2192  </div>\n    42\u2192  <div id=\"wrap\">\n    43\u2192    <canvas id=\"main\"></canvas>\n    44\u2192    <canvas id=\"label-canvas\"></canvas>\n    45\u2192  </div>\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192  <script type=\"module\">\n    48\u2192    import {\n    49\u2192      TILE_SIZE, MAP_COLS, MAP_ROWS, MAP_W, MAP_H,\n    50\u2192      MAP, TILE_COLORS, PROPS, T,\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
  }
}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #137
The debug.html is a static scene renderer - it doesn't have the simulation engine. This version of the game doesn't expose the engine on `window`. Looking at the existing Vercel deployment tests, they use `window._gameEngine`. In the local version, the engine is `engine` (module-scoped). I need to write tests that work against this local dev server. My options are: 1. Inject the engine into `window` via `page.addInitScript()` or `page.evaluate()` 2. Use the debug dashboard's event log to test facing behavior 3. Use a different approach to access game state Actually, the task says the game exposes `window.__SIM_ENGINE__`. Maybe this is a feature of the FRG-244 branch specifically. Let me check if the engine.js in this branch exposes itself:

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #142
Neither `window.__SIM_ENGINE__` nor `window._gameEngine` is exposed in this branch. The existing tests were built for the Vercel deployment where `window._gameEngine` was set. In this local version, `engine` is module-scoped. I have a few options for the tests: 1. **Intercept the engine by page.addInitScript**: Modify the engine exposure via playwright's `addInitScript`. But the issue is that the engine is created in a module script so it's not accessible before the module runs. 2. **Use page.evaluate with DOM injection**: Add a MutationObserver or monkey-patch the SimulationEngine constructor to expose the instance. 3. **Use page.addInitScript + module interception**: I could intercept the ES module by using Playwright's `route` to modify the source files. 4. **Check if the engine is accessible via some other global**: Maybe `renderer` or `aiEngine` is accidentally exposed, though that seems unlikely. 5. **Use the event log in the existing debug.html**: The task mentions using /debug.html which has an event log, but that's a static scene renderer, not a simulation controller. Actually, I should check what the original Vercel production app looks like versus this local version. The task says to clone the SOURCE_REPO which is `chillkimtest-`... The existing QA suite runs against the Vercel deployment, but these new FRG-244 tests need to run against the local server instead. Since `window._gameEngine` isn't exposed here, I need a different strategy. Looking back at the test plan, it confirms characters have properties like `facing`, `tx`, `ty`, `action`, and `targetCharId` that match what I found in the code. The cleanest solution is to use Playwright's `page.route()` to intercept the index.html module request and inject code that exposes `window._gameEngine`. I'm considering a few approaches: using `page.addInitScript()` with a MutationObserver, or `page.exposeFunction()` to create a bridge. Actually, the most straightforward method would be to intercept the HTML request and inject the engine assignment before the closing script tag. Or I could use `page.addInitScript()` to override the SimulationEngine class so it automatically stores itself on the window object when instantiated, which should work even with ES modules since the script runs before page scripts execute. Looking at the existing tests, they were written against the Vercel deployment where `window._gameEngine` is already exposed. The local source doesn't have this, so I could either modify my tests to work with the local setup, or just run them against the same Vercel deployment URL with the new FRG-244 feature. But wait—the feature is still in a branch, so it shouldn't be deployed there yet. Let me think through the actual options: I could inject the engine into the window using Playwright's page routing, or I could try accessing it through `page.evaluate`, though that won't work since ES module variables aren't in the global scope. Let me look more closely at how the existing tests are actually accessing the engine. The existing tests poll for `window._gameEngine`, which means the local server needs to set it for those tests to pass. But looking at the environment config, there's no BASE_URL override—the existing tests are still hitting the Vercel deployment while the new FRG-244 tests need to run against the local server instead. I need to figure out how to expose the game engine to the new tests. I could intercept the engine.js or index.html files to inject the engine into the window, or find a completely different approach that doesn't rely on `window._gameEngine` at all. Let me check why the Vercel deployment has this exposed in the first place. The existing test helper waits for `window._gameEngine` to be available with the right properties, which means the Vercel deployment must be exposing it somehow. I should look at whether this is in the main branch or if the feature branch removed it. Since the QA tests are passing on Vercel with all 88 tests working, the engine exposure must be there—but it's missing in my local feature branch clone, which suggests either the feature branch hasn't added it yet or it was stripped out. I'm weighing two approaches: using Playwright's route interception to inject the engine, or finding a completely different testing strategy that doesn't depend on window exposure. I'm examining how the helpers.js file accesses the engine state through `window._gameEngine`, checking whether properties like `characters` and methods like `getSimTimeString()` are actually exposed on the engine instance in the current deployment.

18:    this.objectEngine = new ObjectEngine();
30:    this.objectEngine.addObjects(OBJECT_DEFS);
